concept.name: arts, culture, entertainment and media
concept.definition: All forms of arts, entertainment, cultural heritage and media

concept.name: crime, law and justice
concept.definition: The establishment and/or statement of the rules of behaviour in society, the enforcement of these rules, breaches of the rules, the punishment of offenders and the organisations and bodies involved in these activities

concept.name: disaster, accident and emergency incident
concept.definition: Man made or natural event resulting in loss of life or injury to living creatures and/or damage to inanimate objects or property

concept.name: economy, business and finance
concept.definition: All matters concerning the planning, production and exchange of wealth.

concept.name: education
concept.definition: All aspects of furthering knowledge, formally or informally

concept.name: environment
concept.definition: The protection, damage, and condition of the ecosystem of the planet Earth and its surroundings

concept.name: health
concept.definition: All aspects of physical and mental well-being

concept.name: human interest
concept.definition: Item that discusses individuals, groups, animals, plants or other objects in an emotional way

concept.name: labour
concept.definition: Social aspects, organisations, rules and conditions affecting the employment of human effort for the generation of wealth or provision of services and the economic support of the unemployed.

concept.name: lifestyle and leisure
concept.definition: Activities undertaken for pleasure, relaxation or recreation outside paid employment, including eating and travel.

concept.name: politics and government
concept.definition: Local, regional, national and international exercise of power, the day-to-day running of government, and the relationships between governing bodies and states.

concept.name: religion
concept.definition: Belief systems, institutions and people who provide moral guidance to followers

concept.name: science and technology
concept.definition: All aspects pertaining to human understanding of, as well as methodical study and research of natural, formal and social sciences, such as astronomy, linguistics or economics

concept.name: society
concept.definition: The concerns, issues, affairs and institutions relevant to human social interactions, problems and welfare, such as poverty, human rights and family planning

concept.name: sport
concept.definition: Competitive activity or skill that involves physical and/or mental effort and organisations and bodies involved in these activities
concept.note: Changes: 2018-04-19 definition changed to make it fit better for chess and eSports.

concept.name: conflict, war and peace
concept.definition: Acts of socially or politically motivated protest or violence, military activities, geopolitical conflicts, as well as resolution efforts

concept.name: weather
concept.definition: The study, prediction and reporting of meteorological phenomena

concept.name: arts and entertainment
concept.definition: All forms of arts and entertainment

concept.name: animation
concept.definition: Stories told through animated drawings in either full-length or short format

concept.name: cartoon
concept.definition: Drawings, such as editorial cartoons and comic strips, often using humour or satire

concept.name: cinema
concept.definition: Stories told through motion pictures, such as full-length or short format documentary or fictional features

concept.name: film festival
concept.definition: All forms of national and international film festivals
concept.note: Changes: 2018-04-09 other Broader Term

concept.name: dance
concept.definition: A form of performing art consisting of purposefully selected sequences of human movement

concept.name: ballet
concept.definition: A classical dance form based on formal gestures, steps and postures

concept.name: modern dance
concept.definition: A theatrical dance style based on free-style expression rather than formal gestures

concept.name: traditional dance
concept.definition: Dance style that reflects the life and culture of a specific group of people or region, such as folk and ceremonial dance

concept.name: fashion
concept.definition: Styles and trends in clothing, footwear, accessories, including the design, presentation and wearing of these styles and trends

concept.name: jewellery
concept.definition: Decorative items worn for personal expression and adornment, such as rings, necklaces, earrings

concept.name: literature
concept.definition: The telling of a story through written language

concept.name: drama (literature)
concept.definition: Work written to be performed on the stage

concept.name: fiction
concept.definition: Written works that are not based on fact, but are the creation of the author's imagination

concept.name: non-fiction
concept.definition: Written works that are based on fact

concept.name: poetry
concept.definition: Written works that use the aesthetic and rhythmic qualities of language to express a message or emotion

concept.name: music
concept.definition: A form of performing art using instruments or voice to create different sounds, tones and harmonies

concept.name: musical instrument
concept.definition: Objects used to play music on

concept.name: musical performance
concept.definition: Musicians performing for an audience for the purpose of entertainment

concept.name: musical genre
concept.definition: A distinguishable type of music

concept.name: classical music
concept.definition: A genre of music rooted in the traditions of Western music, including both religious and secular music, such as symphony, concerto and sonata.

concept.name: country music
concept.definition: A genre of music that originated in the southern United States, often consisting of ballads or dance tunes and featuring string instruments such as banjos, fiddles and guitars

concept.name: folk music
concept.definition: A genre of music that developed from traditional popular cultures, often based on story telling

concept.name: jazz music
concept.definition: A genre of music with roots in blues and ragtime that originated in African American communities of New Orleans in the United States in the late 19th and early 20th centuries

concept.name: popular music
concept.definition: A genre of music that is widely accessible and designed to appeal to all

concept.name: rock and roll music
concept.definition: A genre of music that originated in the United States during the late 1940s and early 1950s, evolving from African American musical styles such as gospel, jump blues, jazz, boogie woogie, and rhythm and blues.

concept.name: opera
concept.definition: A form of performing art where classically trained musicians and singers perform a musical and dramatic work
concept.note: Changes: 2018-04-09 other Broader Term

concept.name: theatre
concept.definition: A form of performing art where live performers portray a narrative to an audience through dialogue and action

concept.name: music theatre
concept.definition: A form of performing art where live performers portray a narrative to an audience through song, dialogue and action, such as musicals and operettas
concept.note: Retired on 2021-09-30. Child terms moved up to be children of medtop:20000029 theatre.

concept.name: visual arts
concept.definition: A form of art which appeals primarily to the visual senses, such as ceramics, drawing, painting, sculpture, printmaking, design, crafts, photography, video, filmmaking, and architecture.

concept.name: architecture
concept.definition: The design of buildings, monuments and the spaces around them

concept.name: design (visual arts)
concept.definition: An artistic discipline which focuses on visual communication and presentation, the final product of which can range from jewellery to interior design

concept.name: drawing
concept.definition: A form of visual arts using mediums such as pencils, markers, pen and ink, charcoal

concept.name: painting
concept.definition: A form of visual arts using mediums such as oil paint, watercolour, pastel, crayon

concept.name: photography
concept.definition: A form of visual arts using photographic images

concept.name: sculpture
concept.definition: A form of visual arts using mediums such as clay, stone, wood and metal to create three-dimensional works

concept.name: culture
concept.definition: The ideas, customs, arts, traditions of a particular group of persons

concept.name: cultural development
concept.definition: The history of the development of art and culture such as the rise of cave paintings, linguistic variations over time, the evolution of values

concept.name: customs and tradition
concept.definition: A particular way of behaving, or observances that have developed over time by a group of people

concept.name: festive event (culture)
concept.definition: An event ordinarily celebrated by a community and centring on some characteristic aspect of that community and its religion or traditions

concept.name: language
concept.definition: The method of human communication, either spoken or written, consisting of words that are used in a structured and conventional way

concept.name: library and museum
concept.definition: Institutions that house collections of books, music, art, or objects from the past and present optionally for public use and display

concept.name: monument and heritage site
concept.definition: Commemorations of historical people, events, or the areas containing them, in the form of structures such as sculptures, statues or buildings

concept.name: mass media
concept.definition: Media addressing a large audience

concept.name: news media
concept.definition: The presentation of news to the public through different platforms, such as TV, radio, newspapers, magazines, web pages and blogs

concept.name: newspaper
concept.definition: Daily or weekly publication that presents the news, locally, regionally or globally, as well as features, opinion columns, comics and other content

concept.name: online media outlet
concept.definition: Publication distributing content, such as articles, blogs, photos, video and music, only over the internet

concept.name: periodical
concept.definition: Publication that is usually published weekly, bi-weekly, monthly or annually, such as magazines, journals or newsletters

concept.name: radio
concept.definition: Audio content, such as news, entertainment or information, distributed via broadcast or internet

concept.name: television
concept.definition: Video content, such as news, entertainment or information, distributed via broadcast or internet

concept.name: act of terror
concept.definition: Act of violence, often resulting in casualties, designed to raise fear and anxiety in a population

concept.name: act of bioterrorism
concept.definition: Attacks using biological agents intended to raise the level of fear within a population

concept.name: terrorist bombings
concept.definition: Intentional use of explosive devices on people, buildings or other structures

concept.name: armed conflict
concept.definition: Disputes between opposing groups involving the use of weapons, but not necessarily formally declared wars

concept.name: guerrilla activity
concept.definition: Anti-government or wartime actions by clandestine or non-state combatant groups, often using tactics such as ambush, sabotage, raids and kidnapping

concept.name: international military intervention
concept.definition: The temporary use of international forces in a country or region that requires outside help with resolving a crisis

concept.name: peacekeeping force
concept.definition: The military forces of a nation, or of several nations working together, whose mission is to maintain peace in areas of conflict

concept.name: military occupation
concept.definition: The temporary forceful taking over of a country or region by invading military forces

concept.name: missing in action
concept.definition: Civilians or military personnel who have gone missing during or after hostilities, conflicts or war

concept.name: war
concept.definition: Armed hostilities by one group or geopolitical entity against another

concept.name: civil war
concept.definition: Armed conflict between members of the same country, in some cases with the goal of dividing the country

concept.name: war crime action
concept.definition: Actions alleged as crimes committed during a war or armed conflict, usually against civilians or POWs
concept.note: 2016-04-05: As two concepts with very similar definitions exist this concept (medtop:20000064) was retired and it is recommended to use war crime (medtop:20000105)

concept.name: civil unrest
concept.definition: Rallies, strikes, demonstrations, riots or other disturbances by a population, often in protest to a government or other organization's policies or actions

concept.name: demonstration
concept.definition: A non-violent public show of support for or opposition to a cause, idea or policy, in the form of a mass meeting or march

concept.name: rebellion
concept.definition: Organized actions of opposition to a government or ruling party by a country's residents, often violent and with the aim of overthrowing the government

concept.name: revolution
concept.definition: A forcible and often violent change to, or overthrow of, a country's political system or social order by internal forces, in favour of a new system

concept.name: riot
concept.definition: Violent, destructive events of civil disorder by groups of people, usually in response to a grievance or as acts of opposition, often involving injury to individuals and destruction of property

concept.name: coup d'etat
concept.definition: The overthrow of an established government by an organised military or political group

concept.name: massacre
concept.definition: The mass killing of people, usually civilians, during wartime hostilities

concept.name: genocide
concept.definition: The systematic killing of one clan, tribe, or religious or ethnic group by another

concept.name: peace process
concept.definition: Organized actions to end a war or conflict, usually involving peace negotiations and agreements

concept.name: peace envoy
concept.definition: Person or organisation initiating and carrying out a peace process

concept.name: peace plan
concept.definition: A strategy for achieving peace in a warring nation or area of conflict

concept.name: peace talks
concept.definition: Discussions between countries, world regions or geopolitical entities in conflict or at war, often with a mediator, with the goal of arriving at a solution for peace

concept.name: post-war reconstruction
concept.definition: Actions to rebuild a society, economy or political system in an area affected by war

concept.name: disarmament
concept.definition: The abolishment or reduction of a country's military weapons cache

concept.name: ordnance clearance
concept.definition: The removal or neutralisation of ordnance such as landmines or cluster bombs that may remain after a war or armed conflict on public ground

concept.name: prisoners of war
concept.definition: Individuals captured, imprisoned or detained by enemy factions during military conflicts or war

concept.name: crime
concept.definition: Violations of laws by individuals, companies or organisations

concept.name: arson
concept.definition: The intentional setting of fires with criminal intent

concept.name: assault
concept.definition: Physical crime against a person, including battery, brawls and threat of bodily harm

concept.name: sex crime
concept.definition: Sexual crimes against a person, such as rape, harassment, molestation or groping

concept.name: cyber crime
concept.definition: Criminal activities carried out by means of computers or the internet, such as hacking, phishing scams and identity theft

concept.name: corporate crime
concept.definition: Crimes committed by a corporation or by individuals acting on behalf of a corporation

concept.name: anti-trust crime
concept.definition: Violations of laws aimed at corporations to promote fair competition for the benefit of consumers

concept.name: breach of contract
concept.definition: The breaking of terms set in a contractual agreement

concept.name: embezzlement
concept.definition: The intentional theft or misuse of money left in one's care or belonging to one's employer

concept.name: insider trading
concept.definition: Trading of stocks by persons having access to internal information not made public at the time

concept.name: restraint of trade
concept.definition: Interference in free competition in business and trade

concept.name: corruption
concept.definition: Dishonest actions by a person in power in return for pecuniary or personal gain

concept.name: bribery
concept.definition: The giving of money or favours in order to influence the judgment or conduct of a person in a position of power

concept.name: drug related crimes
concept.definition: Illegal activities involving illicit substances, including buying, distribution, selling and smuggling

concept.name: drug trafficking
concept.definition: Dealing in illicit often harmful substances

concept.name: fraud
concept.definition: Intentional deception for personal or financial gain

concept.name: hijacking
concept.definition: Taking over a transportation vehicle by force

concept.name: homicide
concept.definition: The killing of one person by another, including murder and manslaughter

concept.name: kidnapping
concept.definition: To seize and detain a person against that person's will by unlawful threat, force or fraud

concept.name: organised crime
concept.definition: Crimes committed by gangs or criminal organizations, such as the mafia

concept.name: gang activity
concept.definition: Criminal activities by groups of individuals, who are allied by common territories, languages or ethnic backgrounds and identify by a common group identity by using a shared name, tattoo, hand sign, clothing style or graffiti symbol

concept.name: terrorism
concept.definition: Violence against people to create fear in order to achieve political or ideological objectives

concept.name: robbery and theft
concept.definition: The taking of another person's property without their consent, sometimes by force

concept.name: war crime
concept.definition: Crimes committed during a war or armed conflict, usually against civilians or prisoners of war, including the prosecution of such crimes

concept.name: judiciary
concept.definition: The system of courts of law

concept.name: court
concept.definition: Place where legal cases are heard and decided

concept.name: appeal (court)
concept.definition: Process in which parties request a formal change to an official decision

concept.name: court administration
concept.definition: Court clerks, bailiffs, stenographers etc.

concept.name: judge
concept.definition: Official who decides cases in a court of law and passes sentences on those found guilty

concept.name: trial (court)
concept.definition: The process by which evidence is presented and guilt, innocence or culpability is determined

concept.name: pre-trial
concept.definition: Actions that happen before the start of a trial, such as pleas, bail motions, discovery and depositions

concept.name: defendant
concept.definition: The person on trial

concept.name: litigation
concept.definition: Taking disputed issues to court for settlement by judge and/or jury

concept.name: witness
concept.definition: A person who testifies

concept.name: out of court procedures
concept.definition: Legal issues which are settled outside of court

concept.name: arbitration and mediation
concept.definition: Resolution of disputed issues by a neutral panel, including conciliation, negotiation and tribunals

concept.name: prosecution and prosecutors
concept.definition: The process of holding a trial against a person who is accused of a crime to determine guilt and the lawyers and legal team who argue that the accused is guilty

concept.name: justice
concept.definition: The equitable administration of laws and regulations
concept.note: Retired. Use a more specific term instead.

concept.name: civil rights
concept.definition: Guarantees of protection to individuals’ freedom from violation by governments, political and social organisations, and other individuals as determined by the laws or constitutions of an individual country

concept.name: law
concept.definition: The codification of rules of behaviour

concept.name: civil law
concept.definition: The system of law focused on private rights and disputes between individuals in such areas as contracts, torts, property, and family law

concept.name: antitrust regulations
concept.definition: laws to promote competition between companies for the benefit of consumers

concept.name: regulations
concept.definition: laws that guide the conduct and organization of business corporations

concept.name: criminal law
concept.definition: The system of law concerned with the punishment of those who commit crimes

concept.name: international law
concept.definition: The system of laws embraced by all nations, such as the Geneva Convention and the International Law of the Seas

concept.name: extradition
concept.definition: Legal transfer of criminals or suspects between countries

concept.name: international court and tribunal
concept.definition: The activities of courts under the authority of international organizations or formed by treaties between nations, such as the European Court for Human Rights and International Court of Justice

concept.name: law enforcement
concept.definition: Agencies that attempt to prevent disobedience to established laws or bring to justice those who disobey those laws

concept.name: arrest
concept.definition: The detention of a suspect of a crime

concept.name: investigation (criminal)
concept.definition: Inquiry into an alleged crime, including searching, interviews, interrogations and evidence collection

concept.name: missing person
concept.definition: An adult whose disappearance is possibly not voluntary, or a child whose whereabouts are unknown to the child's legal guardian

concept.name: police
concept.definition: Civil force of a national, regional or local government that is responsible for the prevention and detection of crime and the maintenance of public order

concept.name: sentencing (criminal)
concept.definition: The retribution handed out to those who break laws

concept.name: capital punishment
concept.definition: The legally authorised execution of someone as punishment for a crime

concept.name: fine (penalty)
concept.definition: Money that a court of law or other authority decides has to be paid as punishment for a crime or other offence

concept.name: prison
concept.definition: Facility where people are held as a punishment for a crime they have committed or while awaiting trial

concept.name: accident and emergency incident
concept.definition: A sudden, unexpected event that causes unwanted consequences or requires immediate action
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: Concept medtop:20000160 was merged into this one as their semantics were not sufficiently different

concept.name: explosion accident and incident
concept.definition: Explosions caused by an accidental, non-combat, or non-terrorism source. Also use when the source of the explosion is non-specified or unknown
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: English name changed

concept.name: industrial accident and incident
concept.definition: Any unplanned event or mishap in an industrial setting that result in injuries to people and damage to property or the environment
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: English name changed

concept.name: nuclear accident and incident
concept.definition: Any unplanned event that causes unwanted consequences involving radioactive materials
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: English name changed

concept.name: transportation accident and incident
concept.definition: An accident or incident involving one or more vehicles
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: Concept medtop:20000162 was merged into this one as their semantics were not sufficiently different

concept.name: air and space accident and incident
concept.definition: An accident or incident involving aircrafts or spacecrafts
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: Concept medtop:20000163 was merged into this one as their semantics were not sufficiently different

concept.name: maritime accident and incident
concept.definition: An accident or incident involving marine vessels.
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: Concept medtop:20000164 was merged into this one as their semantics were not sufficiently different

concept.name: railway accident and incident
concept.definition: An accident or incident involving trains.
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: Concept medtop:20000165 was merged into this one as their semantics were not sufficiently different. Note 2016-08-24: typo in name fixed

concept.name: road accident and incident
concept.definition: An accident or incident involving road vehicles.
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: Concept medtop:20000166 was merged into this one as their semantics were not sufficiently different

concept.name: disaster
concept.definition: A sudden, unplanned event that causes great damage or loss of life, such as an accident or a natural catastrophe

concept.name: famine
concept.definition: Severe lack of food for a large population

concept.name: fire
concept.definition: Fires started by an accidental or unknown source. For wildfires, use wildfire. For fires started with a criminal intent, use arson.

concept.name: natural disaster
concept.definition: Destructive incidents caused by nature, such as earthquakes and floods

concept.name: drought
concept.definition: A severe lack of water over a period of time

concept.name: earthquake
concept.definition: The shifting of the tectonic plates of the Earth, creating in some cases damage to structures

concept.name: flood
concept.definition: Surfeit of water, caused by heavy rains or melting snow, usually in places where it's not wanted

concept.name: landslide
concept.definition: Sudden dislodging of massive amounts of snow or soil

concept.name: avalanche
concept.definition: A sudden fall of snow, ice, rock, or mud down the side of a mountain

concept.name: meteorological disaster
concept.definition: A weather-related disaster

concept.name: windstorms
concept.definition: A storm with very strong wind but little or no rain or snow

concept.name: volcanic eruption
concept.definition: A rupture in the crust of the Earth allowing molten material to escape to the surface

concept.name: emergency incident
concept.definition: A sudden, unexpected event that requires immediate action to prevent serious consequences
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: This concept was merged into concept medtop:20000139 as their semantics were not sufficiently different

concept.name: structural failure
concept.definition: The unexpected collapse of a building, bridge or other structure
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: broader term changed

concept.name: transport incident
concept.definition: An incident involving one or more vehicles
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: This concept was merged into concept medtop:20000143 as their semantics were not sufficiently different

concept.name: air and space incident
concept.definition: Incident involving craft in air or space.
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: This concept was merged into concept medtop:20000144 as their semantics were not sufficiently different

concept.name: maritime incident
concept.definition: Incident involving marine vessels.
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: This concept was merged into concept medtop:20000145 as their semantics were not sufficiently different

concept.name: railway incident
concept.definition: Incident involving trains.
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: This concept was merged into concept medtop:20000146 as their semantics were not sufficiently different

concept.name: road incident
concept.definition: Incident involving road vehicles.
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: This concept was merged into concept medtop:20000147 as their semantics were not sufficiently different

concept.name: emergency planning
concept.definition: Planning for actions to deal with sudden, unplanned events
concept.note: 2019-12-11: Retired. Use medtop:20000168 emergency response instead

concept.name: emergency response
concept.definition: The planning and efforts made by people and organizations to help victims of a sudden, unplanned event, natural disaster or crisis

concept.name: business information
concept.definition: Information about individual business entities, including companies, corporations, charities

concept.name: business finance
concept.definition: A business entity's finances
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000183 business financing instead.

concept.name: accounting and audit
concept.definition: Changes regarding applicable accounting rules and the audit of accounting
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001365 business reporting and performance or medtop:20000272 accountancy and auditing instead.

concept.name: analysts comment
concept.definition: Stock analyst comments, analysis and recommendations.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000396 stocks and securities and/or genre:Analysis instead.

concept.name: bankruptcy
concept.definition: Organisations that cannot pay their debts and file for special treatment by governments and financial systems

concept.name: stock buyback
concept.definition: Corporate actions in which companies buy back their own shares

concept.name: credit rating
concept.definition: Formal statements by certain rating agencies on the investment risk of a company or enterprise

concept.name: corporate dividends
concept.definition: Distribution of company profits to shareholders via dividend payments

concept.name: corporate earnings
concept.definition: Announcements and discussions of corporate earnings

concept.name: earnings forecast
concept.definition: Analyst prediction of company earnings.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000178 corporate earnings instead.

concept.name: financial statement
concept.definition: Formal statements about the company financials typically delivered annually or quarterly

concept.name: proxy filing
concept.definition: Filings with regulatory agencies of proxy statements for upcoming shareholder votes.

concept.name: financially distressed company
concept.definition: Indications that a company is financially distressed but not having filed for bankruptcy yet

concept.name: business financing
concept.definition: The financing, capital structuring, and investment decisions of a business

concept.name: restructuring and recapitalisation
concept.definition: The sale of company units, splits of existing companies into units under a holding company, etc.
concept.note: Use medtop:20001366 business restructuring instead.

concept.name: shareholder activity
concept.definition: Actions taken by owners of one or more shares of a company, including shareholder activism

concept.name: stock activity
concept.definition: Announcements of major stock events: splits, block trades, new highs or lows, etc.

concept.name: stock flotation
concept.definition: The issuing of stock shares to the public for the first time, also called an Initial Public Offering (IPO)

concept.name: human resources
concept.definition: People working for a business and how they are managed

concept.name: layoffs and downsizing
concept.definition: Planned or actual reductions in the labour force.

concept.name: executive officer
concept.definition: Company executive officers including appointments and changes

concept.name: stock option
concept.definition: An agreement to either buy or sell a stock when it meets a certain price, sometimes used as an incentive for employees and officers of a company
concept.note: Retired. Use derivatives medtop:20000395 instead.

concept.name: business strategy and marketing
concept.definition: Planning the direction of a company and selling its products and services to achieve the strategy

concept.name: annual and special corporate meeting
concept.definition: News surrounding company reports and annual or special corporate meetings

concept.name: annual report
concept.definition: Availability or contents of annual company reports.

concept.name: board of directors
concept.definition: Appointments to or changes in the board of directors for a company.

concept.name: commercial contract
concept.definition: Contracts regarding commercial activities

concept.name: company spin-off
concept.definition: The creation of new companies being spun off from existing companies

concept.name: economic globalisation
concept.definition: Doing business around the world

concept.name: business governance
concept.definition: The rules, practices, and processes used to oversee and manage a company

concept.name: joint venture
concept.definition: Two or more companies agreeing on a venture.

concept.name: leveraged buyout
concept.definition: Acquiring control over a company by buying its shares with borrowed money.

concept.name: licensing agreement
concept.definition: The document defining the terms for licensing intellectual property
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000196 commercial contract instead.

concept.name: management buyout
concept.definition: Acquiring control over a company by the management buying its shares.

concept.name: merger or acquisition
concept.definition: Two or more companies forming a new company mergers, takeovers or acquisitions.

concept.name: new product or service
concept.definition: The introduction of products or services to the market

concept.name: patent, copyright and trademark
concept.definition: Intellectual properties such as patents, copyright and trademarks

concept.name: product recall
concept.definition: A decision by a company to take back or repair a defective product

concept.name: research and development
concept.definition: Research and expenditure on new product or service development

concept.name: products and services
concept.definition: Products and services consumed by companies and individuals and the companies that manufacture or manage them

concept.name: agriculture
concept.definition: The growing and raising of plants and animals for consumption and the processing, cleaning, packing or storage of these products

concept.name: aquaculture
concept.definition: Growing plants or animals in water for human consumption
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000210 agriculture instead.

concept.name: arable farming
concept.definition: The growing of plants on a large scale commercially, especially a cereal, fruit, grain or vegetable
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000210 agriculture instead.

concept.name: commercial fishing
concept.definition: Fish farming and commercial activity around the catching of fish and seafood for consumption

concept.name: forestry and timber
concept.definition: The production, collection and preparation of wood products

concept.name: livestock farming
concept.definition: Raising of animals for their by-products, such as meat, eggs, milk, fur and wool
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000210 agriculture instead.

concept.name: viniculture
concept.definition: Production of wines from the vines to the finished products
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000324 beverage and grocery instead.

concept.name: chemicals
concept.definition: Natural or manmade materials used to produce other materials

concept.name: biotechnology business
concept.definition: The business of using engineering technology to study and solve problems of living organisms

concept.name: fertiliser
concept.definition: Natural or manmade materials used to encourage the growth of plants
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000217 chemicals instead.

concept.name: health and beauty product
concept.definition: Compilation of chemicals and other substances for use in enhancing ones looks or smell
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001248 cosmetics instead.

concept.name: inorganic chemical
concept.definition: The branch of chemistry dealing with minerals and metals
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000217 chemicals instead.

concept.name: organic chemical
concept.definition: The branch of chemistry dealing with human, animal or carbon-based substances
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000217 chemicals instead.

concept.name: pharmaceutical
concept.definition: The production of medicines from various chemicals and natural substances and the research and discovery around new medications

concept.name: plastic
concept.definition: The production of plastics and issues around plastic consumption, including bans, recycling, using less plastics and plastic alternatives

concept.name: computing and information technology
concept.definition: Computers and the transmission of information

concept.name: computer and telecommunications hardware
concept.definition: Physical devices used to provide computer and telecommunication service, such as modems, keyboards, hard drives, telephones, routers and monitors

concept.name: computer networking
concept.definition: The interconnectivity between hardware that provides the medium for the transmission of data.

concept.name: satellite technology
concept.definition: Hardware and software that enables ground-based devices to send signals to each other via an orbiting satellite.

concept.name: computer security
concept.definition: To encompass technical developments in hardware and software, basic research and related areas such as cyber security
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000086 cyber crime or medtop:20001361 cyber warfare instead.

concept.name: semiconductor and electronic component
concept.definition: The basic components that together create working electronic devices such as computer chips, transistors, diodes and integrated circuits

concept.name: software and applications
concept.definition: Computer programs including mobile or web apps and operating systems

concept.name: telecommunication equipment
concept.definition: Devices such as telephone sets, voice/data switches, modems etc that facilitate point-to-point voice/data transmissions
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000226 computer and telecommuncations hardware instead.

concept.name: telecommunication service
concept.definition: Services provided by commercial companies that facilitate connections between telephones and computers

concept.name: wireless technology
concept.definition: Technology and services that permit the transfer of information between separated points without physical connection, including Bluetooth and Wi-Fi networks

concept.name: construction and property
concept.definition: All items pertaining to the construction and sale of property

concept.name: design and engineering
concept.definition: Design and planning by engineers, architects etc. of roads, offices, factories, dams , offshore structures.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001245 infrastructure projects instead.

concept.name: farms
concept.definition: Agricultural areas for the production of foodstuffs, including dairy products, fruits and livestock, such as cattle and fish
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000210 agriculture instead.

concept.name: heavy construction
concept.definition: Large scale construction projects, construction of roads, dams, office/factory buildings, offshore and onshore structures, large sport stadiums
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000235 construction and property instead.

concept.name: house building
concept.definition: Construction of residences, for private use
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001368 residential real estate instead.

concept.name: land price
concept.definition: The price of land in specific zoned areas such as commercial, residential or arable as provided by a responsible body
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001368 residential real estate instead.

concept.name: real estate
concept.definition: The buying, selling, renting and leasing of properties of all types
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001368 residential real estate or medtop:20001367 commercial real estate instead.

concept.name: restoration
concept.definition: Restoring historic or important properties or structures to a former better state by cleaning, repairing or rebuilding

concept.name: consumer goods
concept.definition: Items produced for and sold to individuals

concept.name: beverage
concept.definition: Liquid consumables, both alcoholic and non-alcoholic
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000324 beverage and grocery instead.

concept.name: clothing
concept.definition: The manufacturing and purchasing of apparel

concept.name: department store
concept.definition: Store selling a variety of goods, such as clothing, housewares, jewellery and cosmetics

concept.name: online shopping
concept.definition: Buying and selling items through the internet

concept.name: grocery
concept.definition: The selling of edible goods for human consumption to the end customers
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000324 beverage and grocery instead.

concept.name: luxury good
concept.definition: Non-essential high-end goods, such as leather bags, jewellery or supercars

concept.name: mail order
concept.definition: Items sold through and delivered by mail
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000247 online shopping instead.

concept.name: non-durable good
concept.definition: Products meant to only be used for a short period of time
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000243 consumer goods instead.

concept.name: retail
concept.definition: The selling of goods directly to consumers

concept.name: specialty store
concept.definition: Retail outlets that specialise in categories such as shoes, coats, power tools, etc

concept.name: toy and game
concept.definition: Children's playthings or collectible items for adults, such as dolls, action figures and miniatures

concept.name: wholesale
concept.definition: The first link in the sales chain after production

concept.name: energy and resource
concept.definition: The use of natural resources to generate energy

concept.name: renewable energy
concept.definition: Energy derived from sustainable sources

concept.name: coal
concept.definition: Production and mining of anthracite and bituminous products for use in power production

concept.name: diesel fuel
concept.definition: A distilled petroleum product heavier than gasoline used for trucks, marine engines, certain cars and in some forms for home heating

concept.name: electricity
concept.definition: The power line distribution system and the sale of electrical power at wholesale and retail levels

concept.name: energy industry
concept.definition: The energy industry, not focused on any specific sector

concept.name: kerosene/paraffin
concept.definition: A light petroleum distillate used for cooking fuel, aircraft jet engines, lamps, heating and cleaning
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001243 oil and gas instead.

concept.name: natural gas
concept.definition: A natural resource, mainly methane in a flammable gaseous state extracted from the ground

concept.name: nuclear power
concept.definition: Use of radioactive materials for power production

concept.name: oil and gas - downstream activities
concept.definition: All matters concerning oil and gas, typically refining and distribution activities

concept.name: oil and gas - upstream activities
concept.definition: All matters concerning oil and gas, typically supply chain activities from the reservoir to the refinery gate

concept.name: petrol
concept.definition: Distilled petroleum product used for automotive fuel, with or without additives

concept.name: waste management
concept.definition: The collecting, disposing or recycling of household and business waste

concept.name: water supply
concept.definition: The business of providing water for household and commercial use

concept.name: financial and business service
concept.definition: Services that transmit, safeguard, keep track of money or provide backup to commercial enterprises
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001370 financial service or medtop:20001371 business service instead.

concept.name: accountancy and auditing
concept.definition: Companies that provide balance sheet preparation and budget reconciliation services and examine accuracy of financial statements

concept.name: auction
concept.definition: Auction houses and all types of auctions, such as police auctions and silent auctions

concept.name: banking
concept.definition: Financial institutions and services for storing, transmitting, receiving and delivering money

concept.name: consultancy
concept.definition: Providers of expert knowledge in a wide range of fields usually on a temporary, contract basis

concept.name: employment agency
concept.definition: A service helping people find jobs, and companies to find workers

concept.name: funeral parlour and crematorium
concept.definition: Companies that provide services for disposal of the dead
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001235 funeral and memorial service instead.

concept.name: health care provider
concept.definition: Organisations and individuals that provide medical services at all levels

concept.name: insurance
concept.definition: A risk-taking venture that allows individuals to pay small amounts periodically to guard financially against unexpected events

concept.name: financial advisory service
concept.definition: Financial advisers, as opposed to stock dealers or consultants

concept.name: janitorial service
concept.definition: Companies that provide cleaning and similar services for homes and businesses.
concept.note: Retired.

concept.name: legal service
concept.definition: Lawyers, notaries and others who help companies and individuals deal with state, federal and local laws
concept.note: 2016-04-04: definition slightly modified.

concept.name: market research
concept.definition: Gathering information about the needs and wants of consumers

concept.name: market trend
concept.definition: Statistically significant consumer behaviour.

concept.name: personal finance
concept.definition: An individual's income and expenses

concept.name: personal income
concept.definition: Money that a person earns and is his/hers to keep or spend

concept.name: personal finance and investment
concept.definition: Investing an individual's savings in the hope of future gains

concept.name: personal service
concept.definition: Consumer service that is intangible e.g. beauty care such as hairdressing
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001249 hairstyles or medtop:20000220 cosmetics instead.

concept.name: printing service
concept.definition: The production of printed matter such as flyers, ads, signs, etc.

concept.name: rental service
concept.definition: Companies that provide items on a short-term rental basis, including motor vehicles, clothing, tools, heavy equipment and other supplies

concept.name: shipping and postal service
concept.definition: Organisations that prepare and transport packages and documents for individuals or companies by any means, including postal services

concept.name: stock broking
concept.definition: The buying and selling of company shares on behalf of individuals or other entities

concept.name: wedding service
concept.definition: Services and products related to the wedding industry
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001236 wedding instead.

concept.name: manufacturing and engineering
concept.definition: Manufacturers of electrical, electronic and mechanical equipment but does not cover civil engineering.

concept.name: aerospace
concept.definition: Companies that assemble or manufacture components for airplanes and space ships.

concept.name: automotive
concept.definition: Companies that manufacture automotive components and assemble automobiles

concept.name: defence equipment
concept.definition: Manufacturers of guns, tanks, ships or other equipment for military and non-military protection services

concept.name: electrical appliance
concept.definition: Makers of large and small electrical goods for use in homes or business

concept.name: heavy engineering
concept.definition: Manufacturers of engineering equipment, such as cranes and bulldozers, for use in major construction projects

concept.name: industrial component
concept.definition: Manufacturers of mechanical, electrical, electronic items needed in the manufacture of other items

concept.name: instrument engineering
concept.definition: Manufacturers of instruments for calibration, measurement of things like speed, pressure, distance

concept.name: machine manufacturing
concept.definition: Manufacturers of turbines, engines, fans, pumps, motors and other components for powered equipment

concept.name: shipbuilding
concept.definition: Manufacturers of ships and submersibles and suppliers for the maintenance and repair of these vessels

concept.name: media and entertainment industry
concept.definition: The various means of disseminating news, information and entertainment to the public

concept.name: advertising
concept.definition: Methods of promoting goods and services to consumers

concept.name: books and publishing
concept.definition: The business of producing and selling books, including e-books and audiobooks

concept.name: film industry
concept.definition: The business of making, selling and distributing films

concept.name: music industry
concept.definition: Recording, production and marketing of music

concept.name: news agency
concept.definition: Commercial agencies for production and distribution of newsworthy events through print, broadcast, video or electronic media
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001372 news industry instead.

concept.name: newspaper and magazine
concept.definition: The business of producing bound and unbound printed paper products for periodic distribution of news items
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001372 news industry instead.

concept.name: online media industry
concept.definition: The business of distributing news and information through computer networks for public consumption
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001372 news industry instead.

concept.name: public relations
concept.definition: The business of managing and disseminating information from an individual or an organization to the public in order to influence their perception

concept.name: radio industry
concept.definition: Companies and organisations involved in the broadcast of radio stations and their content to the public

concept.name: satellite and cable service
concept.definition: The business of transmitting news, entertainment and information via satellite or cable television services

concept.name: television industry
concept.definition: The production and broadcasting of television content

concept.name: metal and mineral mining and refining
concept.definition: The extractive businesses that remove raw materials, including coal, gold iron and copper, from the earth by drilling and pumping, quarrying or mining

concept.name: building material
concept.definition: Manufacturing of the materials used in construction, such as wallboard, lumber and wire

concept.name: precious material
concept.definition: The mining, refining and sale of materials such as gold, silver, precious metals, diamonds and rare earths

concept.name: iron and steel
concept.definition: Mining, refining and sales of iron-based metals
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000316 metal and mineral mining and refining instead.

concept.name: mining
concept.definition: The industry of digging for metals, minerals, raw materials, such as coal, or precious gems, such as diamonds
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000316 metal and mineral mining and refining instead.

concept.name: non-ferrous metal
concept.definition: Mining, refining and sales of copper, zinc, aluminium, and other metals not based on iron
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000316 metal and mineral mining and refining instead.

concept.name: process industry
concept.definition: The business of turning raw materials into useable products
concept.note: Retired. Use more specific terms instead.

concept.name: distiller and brewer
concept.definition: Manufacture of alcoholic beverages
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000324 beverage and grocery instead.

concept.name: beverage and grocery
concept.definition: The production and sale of foodstuffs

concept.name: furnishings and furniture
concept.definition: Manufacture of furniture, wallpaper, paints and fabrics for interior decoration

concept.name: paper and packaging product
concept.definition: Production of paper material for writing, wrapping, printing, or packaging

concept.name: rubber product
concept.definition: Production of rubber-based materials for gloves, protective coatings, hoses and the like

concept.name: soft drinks
concept.definition: Production of non-alcoholic drinks
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000324 beverage and grocery instead.

concept.name: textile and clothing
concept.definition: Production of fabrics for furnishings and fixtures, and for clothing of various types
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000245 clothing instead.

concept.name: tobacco and nicotine
concept.definition: The sale and manufacturing of tobacco products for consumers, including cigarettes, e-cigarettes and vaping cartridges

concept.name: tourism and leisure industry
concept.definition: Commercial organisations and operations dealing with travel and the pursuit of recreational activities

concept.name: casinos and gambling
concept.definition: Organisations providing outlets for betting money on games of chance or animal races

concept.name: hotel and accommodation
concept.definition: The business of providing lodging to travellers

concept.name: recreational and sporting goods
concept.definition: Manufacture of goods for leisure activities

concept.name: restaurant and catering
concept.definition: The business of providing prepared food for customers

concept.name: tour operator
concept.definition: Operators of trips either locally or internationally

concept.name: transport
concept.definition: The means of getting people or goods from one place to another
concept.note: 2016-04-05: definition slightly changed

concept.name: air transport
concept.definition: The movement of passengers and freight by air

concept.name: commuting
concept.definition: The process of getting to and from work, including transport options, travel networks, car pooling.

concept.name: railway transport
concept.definition: The movement of passengers and freight by rail

concept.name: road transport
concept.definition: The movement of passengers and freight by road

concept.name: traffic
concept.definition: Traffic reports and/or warnings

concept.name: waterway and maritime transport
concept.definition: Commercial movement of people or goods via boats, ships and water

concept.name: economy
concept.definition: Production, consumption, distribution and trade activities affecting regions or countries as a whole

concept.name: economic policy
concept.definition: Government-directed policy on the economy including, production, taxes and tariffs

concept.name: economic trends and indicators
concept.definition: The behaviour and performance of the economy as a whole

concept.name: bonds
concept.definition: Corporate or governmental paper representing the loan of money at a fixed rate and for a fixed time to that company or government
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:medtop:20001376 corporate bond or medtop:20000368 government debt for government debt bonds.

concept.name: budgets and budgeting
concept.definition: A method of balancing revenues against expenses
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000607 government budget or medtop:20000287 personal finance and investment instead.

concept.name: business enterprise
concept.definition: Organisations set up to create and sell a product or service

concept.name: central bank
concept.definition: A country's major bank that sets interest rates and provides transfer of funds between commercial banks

concept.name: consumers
concept.definition: Buyers of goods and services
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000352 consumer confidence, medtop:20000243 consumer goods or medtop:20000284 market trend instead.

concept.name: consumer confidence
concept.definition: A measure of consumers' feelings about current and future economic conditions, including prices, inflation and the quality of retail goods, used as an indicator of the overall state of the economy

concept.name: consumer issue
concept.definition: Issues, such as prices, inflation/deflation, quality of goods, at the retail sales level
concept.note: Retired. Use individual product terms or medtop:20000207 product recall instead.

concept.name: credit and debt
concept.definition: Credit: the amount of money available to you, often by loan. Debt: the amount you owe.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000368 government debt, medtop:20000390 debt market or medtop:20000287 personal finance and investment instead.

concept.name: currency
concept.definition: The system of money used by a country, including the value of one currency as measured against another

concept.name: deflation
concept.definition: The decrease in the general price level of goods and services within an economy

concept.name: economic growth
concept.definition: An increase of the gross domestic product (GDP)

concept.name: economic indicator
concept.definition: Certain indexes, such as company inventories, the movement of prices that show whether the economy is improving or declining
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000346 economic trends and indicators instead.

concept.name: gross domestic product
concept.definition: A measure of a region's economic performance, commonly abbreviated as GDP

concept.name: industrial production
concept.definition: Manufacture of durable and non-durable goods

concept.name: inventories
concept.definition: Goods not sold and held by the producer, wholesaler or retailer

concept.name: productivity
concept.definition: A measure of output from production or service

concept.name: economic organisation
concept.definition: Business, industrial and trade associations, societies and other private-sector groups

concept.name: emerging market
concept.definition: Economies of developing nations that are growing and becoming more engaged with global markets

concept.name: employment statistics
concept.definition: Trends and statistics about areas such as job growth, unemployment and wage growth

concept.name: exporting
concept.definition: The producing of manufacturing of goods by a country to be sold elsewhere

concept.name: government aid
concept.definition: Supply of financial and other help by one government, usually to another
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000643 foreign aid instead.

concept.name: government debt
concept.definition: Debt incurred when a country borrows funds to pay for public services and projects that it cannot afford based on the amount of taxes it has raised

concept.name: importing
concept.definition: A country bringing in goods that were produced or manufactured elsewhere

concept.name: inflation
concept.definition: The overall increase in prices and cost of living throughout an economy

concept.name: interest rates
concept.definition: The amount a lender charges a borrower as a percentage of the principal. Often set by a central bank or government.

concept.name: international economic institution
concept.definition: Global institutions working in the domain of international economic cooperation and stability, such as the International Monetary Fund, UNIDO, World Bank and World Trade Organization

concept.name: international trade
concept.definition: Trade of goods and services between nations

concept.name: trade agreements
concept.definition: Agreements between countries or trading blocs for the sale and purchase of goods and services

concept.name: balance of trade
concept.definition: The difference between the financial value of imports and exports

concept.name: trade dispute
concept.definition: International dispute over issues such as trade barriers, cartels and dumping, resolved by the World Trade Organization

concept.name: trade policy
concept.definition: Governmental decisions on tariffs, shipping, embargoes, and the types of goods and services to be imported and exported

concept.name: investments
concept.definition: Speculative purchases of corporate instruments, of commodities, currencies, or parts thereof
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000287 personal finance and investment instead.

concept.name: monetary policy
concept.definition: Government bank directed policy on the amount of money in circulation and the rate at which it can be loaned

concept.name: mortgages
concept.definition: Trends around loans taken out to buy homes, such as the interest rates of mortgages, the amount of people buying property with a mortgage and the ease of getting approved for a mortgage

concept.name: mutual funds
concept.definition: Pools of shares or bonds for sale often grouped by investment intention, such as growth, income or security

concept.name: prices
concept.definition: The monetary value of goods, services or stocks and bonds
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000370 inflation or medtop:20000356 deflation instead.

concept.name: recession
concept.definition: A decrease in gross domestic product over a period of two quarters in a row

concept.name: tariff
concept.definition: Fees placed on imported or exported goods

concept.name: market and exchange
concept.definition: Market for buying and selling stocks, currencies, commodities and other goods

concept.name: commodities market
concept.definition: The market for goods such as cotton, oil, coal and metals

concept.name: energy market
concept.definition: The market for commodities such as electricity, heat, and fuel products

concept.name: metal
concept.definition: Markets for trading base metals such as copper, aluminium, steel, zinc etc

concept.name: soft commodities market
concept.definition: Market for commodities such as cotton, sugar, sorghum, soybeans
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000386 commodity market instead.

concept.name: debt market
concept.definition: Market for debt instruments such as bonds, certificates of deposit, banker's acceptances and Treasury bills

concept.name: foreign exchange market
concept.definition: The market for international currency

concept.name: loan market
concept.definition: The market where financial organisations provide loans to borrowers and sell them on to investors

concept.name: loans and lending
concept.definition: An agreement by an individual or institution to borrow money with an agreed interest rate and time for its return

concept.name: securities
concept.definition: Markets for trading types of company paper such as stocks and bonds
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000396 stocks and securities

concept.name: derivatives
concept.definition: Financial instruments such as futures, swaps and options that depend on an underlying instrument such as a commodity, bond, equity or currency

concept.name: stocks and securities
concept.definition: Financial instruments such as company shares and bonds

concept.name: parents group
concept.definition: Group of parents set up to support educational activities of their children

concept.name: religious education
concept.definition: Instruction by any faith about that faith's principles and beliefs, including Sunday school or preparation for a religious rite, such as a bar mitzvah

concept.name: school
concept.definition: A building or institution in which education is provided

concept.name: primary education
concept.definition: Education and schools designed to provide students with fundamental skills in reading, writing and mathematics and to establish a solid foundation for later learning

concept.name: further education
concept.definition: Any form of education beyond basic education of several levels
concept.note: Retired on 2021-09-30T12:00:00+00:00. Use medtop:20000403 adult and continuing education or medtop:20001216 vocational education

concept.name: adult and continuing education
concept.definition: Education for adults who have left the formal education system that offers new forms of knowledge or skills, including professional certificates and online courses provided by companies such as Codeacademy or Udemy

concept.name: upper secondary education
concept.definition: Education and schools preparing students for tertiary education or providing skills relevant to employment through a curriculum with an increased range of subject options and streams

concept.name: college and university
concept.definition: Institutions of higher learning that provide degrees in a course of study at the undergraduate, postgraduate or doctorate level

concept.name: college
concept.definition: Institution of higher learning, its specific scope is set differently in different countries
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000405 college and university

concept.name: university
concept.definition: Institutions of higher learning capable of providing doctorate degrees
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000405 college and university

concept.name: lower secondary education
concept.definition: Education and schools that build on primary education with a more subject-oriented curriculum. Schools may be called middle schools or junior highs in the United States.

concept.name: early childhood education
concept.definition: Education for children under the national compulsory education age

concept.name: social learning
concept.definition: The learning of social skills and behaviours through the imitation and observation of others. This can include formal and informal instruction around empathy, emotions and mental health.

concept.name: teaching and learning
concept.definition: Either end of the education equation
concept.note: Retired. Use more specific terms instead.

concept.name: curriculum
concept.definition: The courses offered by a learning institution and the regulation of those courses

concept.name: educational testing and examinations
concept.definition: Polices and standards around the testing and assessment of students, including the merits of standardised testing, and testing methods

concept.name: entrance examination
concept.definition: Exams for entering colleges, universities and all other higher and lower education institutions

concept.name: students
concept.definition: Students as a demographic, including student protests and requests for change and trends among students

concept.name: teachers
concept.definition: Teachers as a demographic, including teacher unions, teacher education and training and the criteria for teachers in various locations

concept.name: climate change
concept.definition: All issues relating to extreme changes in climate, including rising global temperature, debate on the causes and solutions of global warming, greenhouse gases and ways to reduce emissions

concept.name: global warming
concept.definition: This category includes all issues relating to global warming including temperature research, remote sensing on temperature trends, debate on global warming, ways to reduce emissions and carbon trading.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000418 climate change instead.

concept.name: conservation
concept.definition: Preservation of the natural world, such as wilderness areas, flora and fauna
concept.note: Use medtop:20000242 restoration for the restoring and conservation of hertiage and historical sites

concept.name: energy saving
concept.definition: Conservation of electrical, and other power sources
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001374 sustainability instead.

concept.name: nature preserve
concept.definition: Land set aside for conservation and preservation of flora and fauna, such as national, state and provincial parks

concept.name: environmental policy
concept.definition: Government programs and policies related to environmental issues, such as pollution, renewable energy and conservation

concept.name: environmental pollution
concept.definition: The contamination of natural resources by harmful substances

concept.name: air pollution
concept.definition: Contaminants affecting the quality of the air

concept.name: environmental clean-up
concept.definition: The removal of hazardous materials from contaminated areas

concept.name: hazardous material
hazardous material
concept.definition: Materials that are harmful to humans or animals if they are exposed to them, such as radiation, poison gases, chemicals, heavy metals, PCBs, mircoplastics and certain plant products

concept.name: waste material
concept.definition: The environmental impact of waste, including recycling efforts

concept.name: water pollution
concept.definition: Contaminants affecting the quality of water

concept.name: natural resource
concept.definition: Assets afforded by nature without human intervention that can be used for various purposes, such as providing energy or industrial materials

concept.name: energy resources
concept.definition: Such resources as coal, gas, wind, sunlight etc., used to generate heat, electricity and power
concept.note: Retired. Use the relevant term from the business branch instead.

concept.name: land resources
concept.definition: That portion of a nation or state that is above water and available for development either for profit or for the general good of the public
concept.note: Retired. Use the relevant term for what is happening on the land, such as commercial real estate, housing and urban planning policy, etc.

concept.name: forest
concept.definition: Open areas of trees either available for public enjoyment, or for commercial purposes

concept.name: mountain
concept.definition: Elevated land masses formed over the ages either by erosion, volcanic eruption, or movement of massive geographical formations called plates

concept.name: population growth
concept.definition: People and their growth and development within a natural setting
concept.note: Retired. Use population and census (medtop:20000774) instead.

concept.name: renewable energy
concept.definition: The environmental impact of renewable energy, including solar, wind, hydro, biomass and geothermal
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000431 energy resources for environmental impacts or medtop:20000257 renewable energy for the general concept.

concept.name: water
concept.definition: Environmental issues about bodies of water, including oceans, lakes, streams and reservoirs, as well as ice, glaciers and forms of precipitation
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000270 water supply, medtop:20000438 ocean, medtop:20000439 river or other more specific term.

concept.name: ocean
concept.definition: The body of salt water that covers nearly three fourths of the surface of the earth

concept.name: river
concept.definition: Moving water areas bounded by land that extend from earth sources and meander through land areas to join with other water areas

concept.name: wetland
concept.definition: Areas generally marshy and not either under water or dry land. Often related to aquifers for water quality and/or wildlife

concept.name: nature
concept.definition: The natural world

concept.name: ecosystem
concept.definition: Interacting organisms, such as plants, animals, and bacteria, and their environment

concept.name: endangered species
concept.definition: Species in danger of disappearing due to factors such as changes in environment, hunting, human encroachment on their environment or changes in climate

concept.name: invasive species
concept.definition: Non-native plants, animals or other organisms that cause harm to native species by outcompeting them, altering native habitats or disrupting ecosystems

concept.name: disease and condition
concept.definition: Any health conditions affecting humans

concept.name: cancer
concept.definition: A serious and often fatal disease caused when normal cells mutate into tumours

concept.name: communicable disease
concept.definition: Diseases that can be transmitted from one person or animal to another

concept.name: epidemic and pandemic
concept.definition: A disease that affects a large number of people within a community, population, region or that has spread to multiple countries or continents across the world

concept.name: plague (disease)
concept.definition: A highly infectious epidemic disease caused by the Yersinia pestis bacteria
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000449 epidemic and pandemic instead.

concept.name: viral disease
concept.definition: Diseases caused by an infection by viruses

concept.name: HIV and AIDS
concept.definition: HIV, a virus that attacks the body's immune system, and AIDS the disease caused by HIV

concept.name: retrovirus
concept.definition: A family of RNA viruses such as those causing leukaemia and AIDS
concept.note: Retired. Use a more specific term or medtop:20000446 disease and condition instead.

concept.name: heart disease
concept.definition: Diseases and conditions affecting the heart such as heart attacks, the narrowing of arteries, arrhythmia or cardiomyopathy

concept.name: illness
concept.definition: Non-specific maladies such as stomach aches, headaches, flat feet, lumbago
concept.note: Retired. Use a more specific term or medtop:20000446 disease and condition instead.

concept.name: injury
concept.definition: Harm caused to the human body by external forces such as falls, weapons or accidents

concept.name: medical condition
concept.definition: An abnormal condition that affects the structure and functioning of the body, such as chronic pain, high blood pressure or osteoporosis

concept.name: mental health and disorder
concept.definition: The psychological well-being of an individual and the diseases and disorders that affect mood, thinking and behaviour as well as actions taken to bring improvement and awareness to these conditions

concept.name: eating disorder
concept.definition: Anorexia, bulimia, overeating and similar illnesses

concept.name: obesity
concept.definition: A condition of body weight generally considered 20 percent above the norm for gender, age, height and bone structure

concept.name: health facility
concept.definition: Facilities used for any kind of health care

concept.name: hospital
concept.definition: Medical facilities for the treatment of illnesses and injury

concept.name: health organisation
concept.definition: Specific health organisations, including professional associations, non-profit and international groups

concept.name: health treatment and procedure
concept.definition: Remedies, therapies, interventions, medications, testing and treatments to prevent, diagnose, manage or improve a health condition

concept.name: diet
concept.definition: Ways of eating to benefit health or treat a condition, such as plant-based, low-salt, high-fibre or gluten-free

concept.name: dietary supplement
concept.definition: Nutrients taken in addition to food to bolster health

concept.name: non-prescription drug
concept.definition: Chemical substances used in the treatment or prevention of physical or mental condition or disease

concept.name: prescription drug
concept.definition: Drugs that can be obtained only with a doctor's authorisation

concept.name: medical test
concept.definition: Diagnostic procedure, such as stress test, blood test, CAT scan or MRI

concept.name: health care approach
concept.definition: Different approaches to medical care that vary in the methods and substances used to treat symptoms

concept.name: herbal medicine
concept.definition: Treatment based on the use of herbs and other plants to cure or alleviate symptoms

concept.name: holistic medicine
concept.definition: Treatment of the whole person including mental and environmental factors in addition to treating symptoms

concept.name: traditional Chinese medicine
concept.definition: The practice of diagnosis, treatment and prevention of disease based on traditional methods developed in Eastern Asia

concept.name: conventional medicine
concept.definition: The practice of diagnosis, treatment and prevention of disease based on modern scientific research

concept.name: physical fitness
concept.definition: The measure of the body's ability to function efficiently and effectively during work and leisure activities.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001239 exercise and fitness instead.

concept.name: preventative medicine
concept.definition: Substances used to prevent diseases or illnesses
concept.note: Retired.

concept.name: vaccine
concept.definition: Medications designed to create immunity to diseases

concept.name: therapy
concept.definition: Treatment of physical, mental or medical conditions by non-surgical means

concept.name: healthcare policy
concept.definition: Government programs and policies related to healthcare such as drug policies, psychiatric care, health care funding and pandemic response

concept.name: government health care
concept.definition: Health care provided by governments at any level

concept.name: Medicaid
concept.definition: A program of the U.S. government to provide health care to needy citizens of all ages
concept.note: This US-specific entity is not appropriate for Media Topics. The general concept of government-provided health care is covered by the parent.

concept.name: Medicare
concept.definition: A program of the U.S. government to provide health care to senior citizens
concept.note: This US-specific entity is not appropriate for Media Topics. The general concept of government-provided health care is covered by the parent.

concept.name: health insurance
concept.definition: Insurance covering medical costs

concept.name: private health care
concept.definition: Health care provided by private organisations

concept.name: medical profession
concept.definition: Profession requiring formal training in the study, diagnosis, treatment, care and prevention of disease, medical condition or injury

concept.name: medical service
concept.definition: Medical support for doctors, including blood tests and other medical tests on individuals, X-rays, CAT scans, MRIs, etc

concept.name: medical specialisation
concept.definition: The different medical specialist areas

concept.name: geriatric medicine
concept.definition: Specialty focusing on ageing and age-related issues including age-related illnesses, medical research, and trends.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001232 elderly care instead.

concept.name: obstetrics/gynaecology
concept.definition: Specialty of female reproductive organs
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001359 pregnancy and childbirth instead.

concept.name: paediatrics
concept.definition: Medical specialisation that focuses on treating the young, including premature babies, infant care, childhood diseases and teenage health and behaviour problems

concept.name: reproductive medicine
concept.definition: Speciality of reproductive technologies like invitro fertilisation
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000784 family planning instead if relevant.

concept.name: medical staff
concept.definition: Doctors, nurses, interns and others in a medical facility

concept.name: non-human diseases
concept.definition: Any harmful or destructive health condition affecting animals, plants or microorganisms.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000494 animal disease or medtop:20000495 plant disease instead.

concept.name: animal disease
