concept.definition: Diseases that affect animals on a wide scale, including zoonotic diseases such as avian influenza
concept.note: If disease spreads to humans at a large scale, tag medtop:20000449 epidemic and pandemic. If the disease is a common animal ailment, tag medtop:20000714 veterinary medicine

concept.name: plant disease
concept.definition: Disorders affecting plants caused by factors such as parasites, fungi, bacteria, physical injuries or environmental factors

concept.name: accomplishment
concept.definition: Achievements by individuals or groups, animals, plants or other objects, such as winning a competitive contest etc
concept.note: 2021-05-05: Retired. Use award and prize (20000498) or record and achievement (20000499) instead.

concept.name: award and prize
concept.definition: The recognition of an achievement in a field in the form of a symbolic item, such as a plaque or trophy or in the form of a monetary gift, such as a cash prize or grant, given by a government or private institution

concept.name: record and achievement
concept.definition: Non-sport achievement by an individual or group that sets a new record, such as most books read or holding one's breath the longest

concept.name: animal
concept.definition: All animals, including pets, wildlife, insects, and livestock

concept.name: ceremony
concept.definition: Rituals, such as dedications, wreath laying or celebrating the launching of a ship
concept.note: See also religious ritual (20000696)

concept.name: people
concept.definition: Individuals or groups with a focus on emotional facets
concept.note: 2021-05-05: Retired. Use more specific terms instead.

concept.name: human mishap
concept.definition: Silly or stupid human errors

concept.name: high society
concept.definition: Life and behaviour of the rich and socialites

concept.name: celebrity
concept.definition: Life and behaviour of famous people

concept.name: royalty
concept.definition: Imperial and royal family members in their societal rather than governmental roles

concept.name: flowers and plants
concept.definition: All plants, including flowers, trees, and shrubs

concept.name: employment
concept.definition: The state of having work, usually paid, for a company, organisation, or individual

concept.name: apprenticeship
concept.definition: A period of work used to learn a skill or trade

concept.name: child labour
concept.definition: The employment of children

concept.name: employee
concept.definition: Those who are paid or otherwise compensated for providing services to employers

concept.name: employer
concept.definition: People and organisations that pay employees to provide services

concept.name: employment training
concept.definition: Learning new skills as part of an existing job, such as developing specialisations, learning new techniques or retraining when an employee's skills are no longer relevant

concept.name: advanced training
concept.definition: Additional training to enhance current skills

concept.name: retraining
concept.definition: Providing a worker training in new skills when his old skills are no longer applicable

concept.name: occupations
concept.definition: Generally one's trade, profession or business although professions are usually such things as lawyers and doctors, and occupations are such as clerks or truckers

concept.name: wages and benefits
concept.definition: Money, or other items of value such as pension contributions and health insurance, paid to employees as compensation for their work

concept.name: employee benefits
concept.definition: Benefits provided to an employee by a company, such as pension contributions, health insurance.

concept.name: social security
concept.definition: a government system that provides monetary assistance to people with inadequate or no incomes, such as retirees, disabled people or carers

concept.name: employment legislation
concept.definition: Laws governing employment

concept.name: workplace health and safety
concept.definition: Laws protecting a safe and healthy work environment

concept.name: labour market
concept.definition: The supply and demand of labour in an economy

concept.name: labour relations
concept.definition: The relationship between workers and employers

concept.name: collective agreements
concept.definition: Negotiated guidelines for relationships between groups of employees and employers, including issues such as working hours, wages, health care, pensions and other benefits

concept.name: contract issue-healthcare
concept.definition: Element of contracts and contract discussions centring on health care issues

concept.name: contract issue-wages
concept.definition: Element of contracts and contract discussions centring on remuneration

concept.name: contract issue-work rules
concept.definition: Element of contracts and contract discussions centring on rules affecting issues such as lunch and other breaks, work shifts, vacation scheduling, seniority, sick leave etc.

concept.name: labour dispute
concept.definition: Disagreements between employers and employees regarding work conditions, pay or other issues

concept.name: labour strike
concept.definition: Groups of employees agreeing to cease work activities with the aim of achieving better pay or work conditions

concept.name: retirement
concept.definition: The years after work

concept.name: pension
concept.definition: Payments, either government or private, to retired people

concept.name: unemployment
concept.definition: The state of being available to work but not having a job

concept.name: job layoffs
concept.definition: The reduction of a company's work force through dismissal because of changing economic factors

concept.name: unemployment benefits
concept.definition: Monetary compensation paid to the jobless

concept.name: unions
concept.definition: Groups established to represent bodies of workers in order to obtain better workplace conditions through collective bargaining

concept.name: leisure
concept.definition: Activities carried out in ones' spare time and not in a competitive way.

concept.name: club and association
concept.definition: Organisations joined by individuals because of similar interests

concept.name: game
concept.definition: Contests generally for one's amusement

concept.name: board game
concept.definition: A game played on a board

concept.name: card game
concept.definition: A game played with a pack of cards

concept.name: children's game
concept.definition: A game designed for and most often played by children

concept.name: dice game
concept.definition: A game played using dice only

concept.name: outdoor game
concept.definition: A game played in an open space

concept.name: puzzle
concept.definition: A game played solving logic problems for entertainment

concept.name: tile game
concept.definition: A game played using tiles

concept.name: video game
concept.definition: A game played using a video display

concept.name: gaming and lottery
concept.definition: Gambling, often involving selection of sets of numbers one expects to come up

concept.name: hobby
concept.definition: Recreational pursuit

concept.name: holiday
concept.definition: Time spent not at work for rest and relaxation

concept.name: public holiday
concept.definition: A day of celebration declared by a government

concept.name: leisure venue
concept.definition: A place where people go to be entertained or amused. For sporting events, use sport venue.

concept.name: amusement park
concept.definition: A park providing various rides and entertainment attractions for both adults and children, including theme parks

concept.name: bar
concept.definition: A business where drinks are prepared and served to the public for consumption on the premises

concept.name: cafe
concept.definition: A small business where drinks and snacks are prepared and served - its specifics may depend on the local culture

concept.name: nightclub
concept.definition: A commercial establishment providing music, or other entertainment along with food and drink to selected clientele

concept.name: restaurant
concept.definition: A business where meals are prepared and served to the public

concept.name: sports facilities
concept.definition: Gymnasiums, stadiums or arenas where sports events take place

concept.name: outdoor recreational activities
concept.definition: Leisure activities engaged in the outdoors, most commonly in natural settings

concept.name: fishing
concept.definition: A recreational activity involving the use of baits, lures, weapons and traps for the capture of aquatic species.

concept.name: hunting
concept.definition: An activity involving the use of weapons or traps for the capturing of animals

concept.name: travel and tourism
concept.definition: Spending time away from home in pursuit of recreation, relaxation or pleasure

concept.name: tourism
concept.definition: Leisure travel involving visits to a number of locations and/or countries over an extended period of time, often as part of an organised group

concept.name: lifestyle
concept.definition: The way in which a person lives, including their style and possessions

concept.name: automobile enthusiasm
concept.definition: Interest in new and classic cars

concept.name: bicycle enthusiasm
concept.definition: Interest in new and classic manpowered cycles

concept.name: food and drink enthusiasm
concept.definition: Interest in eating and drinking

concept.name: organic food
concept.definition: Food which is grown using no artificial fertilisers or chemical compounds

concept.name: house and home
concept.definition: Interest in maintaining and decorating one's home

concept.name: motorcycle enthusiasm
concept.definition: Interest in new and classic motor-powered cycles

concept.name: trend
concept.definition: Interest in what is considered hip or popular at a certain point in time

concept.name: election
concept.definition: The selection of government representatives by the casting of votes by the populace

concept.name: citizens' initiative and recall
concept.definition: Political suggestions by non-government officials for corrective action, or for changes in existing rules and regulations

concept.name: voting system
concept.definition: The set of rules that determine how elections are conducted and how their results are determined, including discussions of the electoral college, ranked choice voting or the allocation of governmental seats

concept.name: intergovernmental elections
concept.definition: Choosing individuals for the leadership of an international organisation, such as the United Nations, European Union, NATO and World Bank

concept.name: local elections
concept.definition: Choosing individuals for government at the city, village or county level

concept.name: national elections
concept.definition: Choosing individuals for government at a national level

concept.name: political campaigns
concept.definition: Campaigns for public office

concept.name: campaign finance
concept.definition: The money that makes campaigns for public office possible

concept.name: political candidates
concept.definition: Individuals who are chosen to stand for office

concept.name: primary elections
concept.definition: Preliminary elections or caucuses to choose candidates to represent a political party in a regional or national election

concept.name: referendum
concept.definition: Political proposals, laws and actions to be voted on by the entire voting body, including ballot measures and citizens' initiatives

concept.name: regional elections
concept.definition: Choosing individuals for government at a regional, state or province level

concept.name: voting
concept.definition: The act of casting a ballot for a candidate, a party or an initiative in a political election, including voting machines, voting by mail or voter turnout

concept.name: fundamental rights
concept.definition: The political, social, cultural and economic rights to which all people are entitled, usually upheld by law

concept.name: censorship and freedom of speech
concept.definition: The right to express opinions without restraint and attempts to supress speech by an organisation or individual

concept.name: freedom of religion
concept.definition: The right to practice the religion of one's choosing and efforts to violate this right through the intimidation of believers or laws that encroach on religious rituals

concept.name: freedom of the press
concept.definition: The right of the press to report on stories freely and efforts by governments, organizations or individuals to suppress, jail or kill journalists who are reporting the news

concept.name: human rights
concept.definition: Rights entitled to be enjoyed by all people universally

concept.name: government
concept.definition: The systems, institutions and people who run a political entity

concept.name: civil and public service
concept.definition: The paid service by civilians for the government, and the often non-paid service of individuals for the benefit of others (public service)
concept.note: 2025-03-31: Retired. Use government employee (medtop:20000595) instead.

concept.name: government employee
concept.definition: Individuals employed by a government at all levels

concept.name: public officials
concept.definition: Individuals who are elected or appointed to positions in the government

concept.name: constitution (law)
concept.definition: A basic framework that stands above all laws and regulations, specifying how a particular political entity is governed

concept.name: national security
concept.definition: The protection of a political entity

concept.name: armed forces
concept.definition: The organizations and individuals that serve as part of the military of a political entity

concept.name: veterans' affairs
concept.definition: Care and provision for those who served in the military

concept.name: military equipment
concept.definition: Equipment issued to members of the armed forces
concept.note: 2025-03-31: Retired. Use military weaponry and equipment (medtop:20000602) instead.

concept.name: military weaponry and equipment
concept.definition: The equipment and weapons used by armed forces and the trade of armaments between political entities

concept.name: security measures (defence)
concept.definition: Means of making a nation, a state, a building or a person secure from harm and outside interference.
concept.note: 2025-03-31: Retired. Use national security (medtop:20000598) instead.

concept.name: national security
concept.definition: The philosophy of securing a nation and its borders, as well as the well-being of its citizens
concept.note: 2025-03-31: Retired. Use national security (medtop:20000598) instead.

concept.name: espionage and intelligence
concept.definition: Covert collection of information

concept.name: executive (government)
concept.definition: Branch of government that is responsible for implementing the laws passed by the legislative branch, sometimes led by a prime minister or president

concept.name: government budget
concept.definition: The proposed expenses and revenues approved by the legislature.

concept.name: public finance
concept.definition: The money of government used for paying for public programmes and services and public debt
concept.note: 2025-03-31: Retired. Use government budget (medtop:20000607) or government debt (medtop:20000368) instead.

concept.name: government department
concept.definition: Divisions of a government that concentrate on specific areas such as health, welfare, economy or war
concept.note: 2025-03-31: Retired. Use more specific terms or government (medtop:20000593) instead.

concept.name: head of state
concept.definition: Symbolic or actual chief representative of a nation, such as a monarch, president or emir

concept.name: impeachment
concept.definition: The process of bringing a public official before a tribunal to answer charges of wrongdoing

concept.name: local authority
concept.definition: Authorities at borough, city or county level

concept.name: minister or secretary (government)
concept.definition: Senior government official, either elected or appointed, who heads a government department or leads an initiative

concept.name: national government
concept.definition: The ruling body of a nation

concept.name: legislative body
concept.definition: A legislature of elected officials representing the people that has the authority to make laws

concept.name: lower house (legislature)
concept.definition: Lower chamber of a legislative body, such as the US House of Representatives or UK House of Commons
concept.note: Note to translators: the examples in the English definition should be converted to relevant examples in countries which speak the target language.

concept.name: upper house (legislature)
concept.definition: Senior chamber of a legislative body, such as the US Senate or UK House of Lords
concept.note: Note to translators: the examples in the English definition should be converted to relevant examples in countries which speak the target language.

concept.name: regional authority
concept.definition: Authorities at a level above the local and below the national, such as a state or province

concept.name: safety of citizens
concept.definition: Government policies to protect the well-being of its citizens
concept.note: Retired. Use other terms such as public health (medtop:20001358) or emergency response (medtop:20000168) instead.

concept.name: taxation policy
concept.definition: Government programmes and policies around the taxation of citizens and entities

concept.name: government policy
concept.definition: An overall plan or course of action set out by a government intended to influence and guide decisions

concept.name: nationalisation
concept.definition: State takeover of private organisations or properties

concept.name: privatisation
concept.definition: The transfer of state-owned organisations or properties to private owners

concept.name: state-owned enterprise
concept.definition: Enterprises owned partially or completely by the government

concept.name: interior policy
concept.definition: Government policies affecting internal affairs
concept.note: Retired. Use more specific terms instead.

concept.name: data protection policy
concept.definition: Efforts to protect personal information in written, oral or electronic form and regulations around what personal information can be collected by the government or other entities

concept.name: housing and urban planning policy
concept.definition: Systematic planning of urban and suburban areas and the housing within those areas

concept.name: policy towards indigenous people
concept.definition: Government progams and policies toward indigenous peoples, such as land allocation, rights protection and financial support

concept.name: pension and welfare policy
concept.definition: Government policies supporting citizens financially through programs such as unemployment payments, pensions and financial support for the poor

concept.name: personal data collection policy
concept.definition: The collection, by government or other entities of information on individuals
concept.note: Retired. Use data protection policy (medtop:20000627) instead.

concept.name: personal weapon control policy
concept.definition: Government policies and regulations around the personal ownership of firearms and other weapons

concept.name: planning inquiries
concept.definition: Public hearings or planning inquiries on proposed constructions, e.g. Construction of water driven power station in a National Park.
concept.note: Retired. Use zoning policy (medtop:20001383), environmental policy (medtop:20000423) or other more specific terms instead.

concept.name: immigration policy
concept.definition: Policies around immigration into a country such as who may immigrate to a country, how many people are allowed in each year, deportation of illegal immigrants and integration programmes that help new immigrants assimilate to a country's culture

concept.name: nuclear policy
concept.definition: Government policies around the production of use of nuclear power and weapons

concept.name: regulation of industry
concept.definition: Government regulation dictating how companies operate

concept.name: food and drink regulations
concept.definition: Government policies relating to the production and selling of food and drink, including ensuring food safety

concept.name: international relations
concept.definition: Relations between nations through negotiation, treaty, or diplomacy

concept.name: diplomacy
concept.definition: Negotiations between representatives of nations or groups of nations and the diplomats who are involved in the talks

concept.name: summit meeting
concept.definition: Meetings between high-level government officials or heads of state for the purpose of diplomatic negotiations to ease international tensions or to discuss common goals

concept.name: treaty
concept.definition: A written agreement between international entities that is binding under international law

concept.name: economic sanction
concept.definition: Economic measures taken by one country or intergovernmental agency against another such as trade restrictions and embargoes

concept.name: foreign aid
concept.definition: Support from one nation to another for purposes such as disaster, general economic development or diplomacy

concept.name: international organisation
concept.definition: Organisation with members and functions that cross international borders

concept.name: refugees and internally displaced people
concept.definition: People seeking shelter or asylum in another region because of some fear of persecution, conflict, or natural disaster in their own region

concept.name: non-governmental organisation (NGO)
concept.definition: Groups officially outside of government that lobby, demonstrate or campaign on a wide range of issues such as economics, the environment and healthcare

concept.name: political crisis
concept.definition: Conflict that rises to a level where, depending on events, governments can fall etc
concept.note: Retired. Use more specific terms instead.

concept.name: political prisoners and dissenters
concept.definition: Individuals who put themselves at risk by expressing their political views and the imprisonment of individuals who speak out against a political authority

concept.name: political process
concept.definition: The art or science of participating in the affairs of government, a state of political party
concept.note: Retired. Use government (medtop:20000593) or other more specific terms instead.

concept.name: lobbying
concept.definition: Advocacy to influence legislators or government officials

concept.name: political parties and movements
concept.definition: Covers both formally recognised and informal political associations
concept.note: Retired. Use political party (medtop:20001384) or political movement and association (medtop:20001385) instead.

concept.name: political development
concept.definition: The creation and rise of political systems and the history of the people and nations that are associated with those systems.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:11000000 politics instead.

concept.name: political system
concept.definition: The form of government that is observed, recognised or otherwise declared by a society or state

concept.name: democracy
concept.definition: Form of government in which the people hold the power, either directly or through elected officials

concept.name: dictatorship
concept.definition: Form of government in which a single individual or a small group of individuals hold power without the consent of the people

concept.name: belief systems
concept.definition: A set of beliefs prescribed by an institution or text often focusing on the worship of a higher power and the outlining of moral guidelines

concept.name: Buddhism
concept.definition: An Asian religion founded in the 6th century BC in India based on the teachings of the Buddha with the goal of overcoming suffering and transcending desire and the individual self to achieve nirvana

concept.name: Christianity
concept.definition: Religions that follow the teachings of Jesus Christ and the Bible

concept.name: ecumenism
concept.definition: Relations between Christian churches
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use Christianity (medtop:20000659) instead.

concept.name: Mormonism
concept.definition: Christian religion founded in New York State in the 1820s that follows the teachings of Joseph Smith and the "Book of Mormon"

concept.name: Old Catholic
concept.definition: Members of a catholic church which started in 1870 and denies the dogma of the infallibility of the pope
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use Catholicism (medtop:20001344) instead.

concept.name: Christian Orthodoxy
concept.definition: Eastern rite churches which are characterised by their continuity with the apostolic church, their liturgy and their territorial churches, mainly in eastern and south-eastern Europe and the Middle East

concept.name: Protestantism
concept.definition: Christian denominations that were formed through the 16th century Reformation movement

concept.name: Anglican
concept.definition: Members of the worldwide Anglican communion which started in the 16th century in England as a breakaway church from the roman catholic church
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use Protestantism (medtop:20000664) instead.

concept.name: Baptist
concept.definition: Members of a Christian church which only baptises adults who truly believe in Christ
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use Protestantism (medtop:20000664) instead.

concept.name: Lutheran
concept.definition: Christians who follow the teaching of reformer Martin Luther
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use Protestantism (medtop:20000664) instead.

concept.name: Mennonite
concept.definition: Members of a 16th century Christian church which is against military service and baptising infants
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use Protestantism (medtop:20000664) instead.

concept.name: Methodist
concept.definition: Followers of John and Charles Wesley who tried to reform the Church of England in the 18th century and turned into a separate church
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use Protestantism (medtop:20000664) instead.

concept.name: Reformed
concept.definition: Christians who follow the teaching of reformers Johannes Calvin and Ulrich Zwingli
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use Protestantism (medtop:20000664) instead.

concept.name: Roman Catholic
concept.definition: Biggest Christian church worldwide tracing its origins back to Jesus Christ
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use Catholicism (medtop:20001344) instead.

concept.name: concordat
concept.definition: Treaty between the Holy See and another country for the regulation of ecclesiastical affairs in the territory of the latter
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use Catholicism (medtop:20001344) instead.

concept.name: Confucianism
concept.definition: A system of thought and behaviour originating in ancient China and based on the teachings of the philosopher Confucius

concept.name: cult
concept.definition: A group that consists of followers who are fully devoted to a charismatic leader. Members are sometimes exploited financially or subject to emotional or physical harm

concept.name: Freemasonry
concept.definition: A secret international society based on the principles of brotherhood, charity and mutual aid
concept.note: Retired in December 2021

concept.name: Hinduism
concept.definition: A religion and social system originating in India that includes a caste system, a belief in reincarnation and the worship of multiple deities

concept.name: Islam
concept.definition: Monotheistic religion that considers Muhammad as a prophet and the Qur'an as holy scripture

concept.name: Jainism
concept.definition: A religion beginning in the 7th-5th century BC in India that teaches a path to spiritual purity and enlightenment through a disciplined mode of life founded upon the tradition of ahimsa, nonviolence to all living creatures

concept.name: Judaism
concept.definition: A monotheistic religion and culture that follows the teachings of the Torah

concept.name: nature religion
concept.definition: Religions that believe nature and the natural world is an embodiment of divinity, sacredness or spiritual power, including indigenous religions practiced in various parts of the world, and modern Pagan faiths, such as Wicca

concept.name: Zoroastrianism
concept.definition: A religion originating in ancient Iran following the teachings of the prophet Zoroaster, predicting the triumph of good over evil and exalting the benevolent deity of wisdom, Ahura Mazda (Wise Lord), as its supreme being

concept.name: Scientology
concept.definition: A set of beliefs and practices invented by American science fiction author L. Ron Hubbard asserting that a human is an immortal, spiritual being (Thetan) that is resident in a physical body

concept.name: Shintoism
concept.definition: An indigenous Japanese polytheistic religion that revolves around kami, supernatural entities believed to inhabit all things

concept.name: Sikhism
concept.definition: An Indian Dharmic religion that originated at the end of the 15th century CE in the Punjab region, developed from the spiritual teachings of Guru Nanak

concept.name: Taoism
concept.definition: A philosophical and spiritual tradition originating in the 4th century BCE in China which emphasizes living in harmony with the Tao, the source, pattern and substance of everything that exists

concept.name: Unificationism
concept.definition: A religion founded in 1954 in South Korea following the teachings of Rev. Syn Myung Moon and known for its mass wedding ceremonies

concept.name: interreligious dialogue
concept.definition: Any form of communication between religions
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use religion (medtop:12000000) instead.

concept.name: religious conflict
concept.definition: Conflicts involving religious differences

concept.name: religious event
concept.definition: News regarding a religious event but not a festival or holiday
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use religion (medtop:12000000) instead.

concept.name: religious festival and holiday
concept.definition: Holy day or period of observance in a religion which is sometimes a public holiday

concept.name: Christmas
concept.definition: Christian holiday that celebrates the birth of Jesus Christ. Is also celebrated by many as a secular holiday.

concept.name: Easter
concept.definition: Christian festival commemorating the resurrection of Jesus Christ. Is also celebrated by many as a secular holiday.

concept.name: Pentecost
concept.definition: Christian festival, celebrated on the 50th day after Easter commemorating the descent of the Holy Spirit, also known as Whitsun

concept.name: Ramadan
concept.definition: Holy month of fasting in Islam concluding in Eid al-Fitr

concept.name: Yom Kippur
concept.definition: Most solemn of Jewish religious holidays, observed on the 10th day of the lunar month of tishri by fasting and prayer from sunset to sundown the following day

concept.name: religious ritual
concept.definition: Established religious rituals such as mass, baptism, prayer meetings and bar and bat mitzvah
concept.note: See also ceremony (20000501)

concept.name: religious facility
concept.definition: Any facility where a group carries out its religious rites

concept.name: church
concept.definition: A building used as a place of worship for the Christian religion

concept.name: mosque
concept.definition: A building used as a place of worship for the Islamic religion

concept.name: synagogue
concept.definition: A building used as a place of worship for the Jewish religion

concept.name: temple
concept.definition: Generally an edifice identifiable as a place of worship
concept.note: Retired in December 2021. Use religious facility (medtop:20000697) instead.

concept.name: relations between religion and government
concept.definition: Matters arising from the relationship between religions and a government, including the question of whether churches should pay taxes, the push to keep religion out of publicly funded institutions and the freedom, or lack of to wear religious symbols in a school setting

concept.name: religious leader
concept.definition: Person or persons who have a ritual, juridical or otherwise leading role in their respective church or religion

concept.name: pope
concept.definition: Head of the Roman Catholic Church worldwide

concept.name: religious text
concept.definition: Texts regarded as holy or important by a religion

concept.name: Bible
concept.definition: Holy book of Christianity

concept.name: Qur'an
concept.definition: Holy book of Islam

concept.name: Torah
concept.definition: Holy book of Judaism

concept.name: biomedical science
concept.definition: The application of biology-based science to medical fields such as research, health monitoring or medical treatment

concept.name: biotechnology
concept.definition: The scientific manipulation of living organisms and biological processes for scientific, medical or agricultural purposes

concept.name: dentistry
concept.definition: The medical profession and field of study devoted to diagnosing, preventing and treating oral conditions

concept.name: pharmacology
concept.definition: The medical profession and field of study concerned with the uses, modes of action and effects of pharmaceutical agents
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000468 prescription drugs instead.

concept.name: veterinary medicine
concept.definition: The medical profession and field of study devoted to diagnosis, prevention and treatment of medical conditions in animals

concept.name: mathematics
concept.definition: The study of structure, space, change and number in abstract, often using symbolic logic and language, and including subjects such as geometry, algebra or trigonometry

concept.name: mechanical engineering
concept.definition: The study and application of the design, construction and operation of mechanical systems

concept.name: natural science
concept.definition: The sciences that deal with matter, energy and the physical world, including physics, biology, chemistry and astronomy

concept.name: astronomy
concept.definition: The study of celestial objects through direct observation and theoretical models

concept.name: biology
concept.definition: The study of the anatomy, behaviour, origin, physiology and other aspects of living organisms

concept.name: botany
concept.definition: The study of the physical and physiological structure, genetics, ecology and other aspects of plants

concept.name: genetics
concept.definition: The study of genes in living organisms, and of their role in heredity, development or genetic adaptation

concept.name: palaeontology
concept.definition: The study of prehistoric life forms, geological periods and fossils

concept.name: physiology
concept.definition: The study of the vital processes, internal organs or functional mechanisms of living organisms

concept.name: zoology
concept.definition: The study of the biology, physiology, development, behaviour or other aspects of animals

concept.name: chemistry
concept.definition: The study of the composition and properties of matter on the scale of atoms and molecules, and of the reactions between compounds

concept.name: cosmology
concept.definition: The study of the origin, evolution, organisation and structure of the universe

concept.name: geology
concept.definition: The study of Earth's physical processes, material structures and its properties

concept.name: horticulture
concept.definition: The study and art of plant cultivation, including landscape and garden design and plant conservation

concept.name: marine science
concept.definition: The study of living organisms and environments in saltwater ecosystems

concept.name: meteorology
concept.definition: The study of atmospheric chemistry and physics, with a focus on weather forecasting

concept.name: physics
concept.definition: The study of the movement and structure of matter, and how it relates to energy

concept.name: electromagnetism
concept.definition: The study of the physical interactions between electrically charged particles and magnetic fields

concept.name: nuclear physics
concept.definition: The study of the structure and behaviour of atomic nuclei

concept.name: particle physics
concept.definition: The study of the nature, properties and interactions of the elementary particles that constitute matter

concept.name: scientific research
concept.definition: The scientific and methodical investigation of events, procedures and interactions to explain why they occur, or to find solutions for problems

concept.name: scientific innovation
concept.definition: The development or creation of new scientific or technological products or processes, such as biotech or artificial intelligence applications, robotic limbs or nanoscale cameras

concept.name: medical research
concept.definition: Investigation conducted in the fields of health and medicine, such as genetic studies, disease research or pharmaceutical drug trials

concept.name: scientific exploration
concept.definition: Land, sea or space journeys undertaken to discover new information

concept.name: space exploration
concept.definition: The scientific exploration of outer space through manned and unmanned missions

concept.name: scientific publication
concept.definition: Academic research findings published in a journal, book or thesis

concept.name: scientific institution
concept.definition: Institution that carries out or governs scientific work, such as the National Academy of Arts and Sciences

concept.name: social sciences
concept.definition: The study of human society in such aspects as linguistics, anthropology, economics or sociology

concept.name: anthropology
concept.definition: The study of human behaviour and social interactions across time

concept.name: archaeology
concept.definition: The study of human activity throughout time and cultural history, using artefacts left behind by ancient peoples

concept.name: economics
concept.definition: The study of the laws and principles of economies, such as the production and distribution of goods and services

concept.name: geography
concept.definition: The study of the physical features of the surface of Earth and its political divisions

concept.name: history
concept.definition: The study of human events of the past

concept.name: information science
concept.definition: The study and practice of collecting, classifying, storing, retrieving and disseminating information

concept.name: study of law
concept.definition: The study of legal systems and the philosophy of law

concept.name: linguistics
concept.definition: The study of all aspects of human language such as syntax, phonetics, written and spoken forms and variations over time

concept.name: philosophy
concept.definition: The study of the principles and fundamental questions about matters such as the mind, reason, morality or values

concept.name: political science
concept.definition: The study of the principles of political and governmental systems, political thought and practices

concept.name: psychology
concept.definition: The study of the human mind, mental characteristics and emotional processes

concept.name: sociology
concept.definition: The study of human social organisation, social relationships and societal changes

concept.name: scientific standards
concept.definition: Nationally or internationally established rules governing scientific and technological study and development, such as calibration standards for scientific tools or equipment

concept.name: technology and engineering
concept.definition: The study and practice of industrial or applied sciences such as physics, hydrodynamics or thermodynamics

concept.name: aerospace engineering
concept.definition: The study, design, development and construction of aircraft, spacecraft and missile systems

concept.name: rocketry
concept.definition: The study, design, development and construction of rockets

concept.name: agricultural technology
concept.definition: The study and development of techniques and machinery to improve agricultural management and productivity

concept.name: civil engineering
concept.definition: The study, design, development and construction of structures such as buildings, bridges, tunnels, irrigation and sewage systems, streets or railroads

concept.name: electronic engineering
concept.definition: The study, design, development and application of systems built on the exchange of electrical charges, such as consumer devices, communications systems or industrial computers

concept.name: identification technology
concept.definition: The study, development, application and ethical considerations of technological methods for identifying products, animals or people; including radio frequency, biometrics or face recognition

concept.name: information technology and computer science
concept.definition: The study and design of computer systems, software and networks

concept.name: materials science
concept.definition: The study of the composition, structure and properties of materials for their use in industry and manufacturing

concept.name: micro science
concept.definition: The study of and technologies built at a microscopic scale, such as microbiology or microcomputing

concept.name: nanotechnology
concept.definition: Manipulating objects at the molecular scale

concept.name: communities
concept.definition: A group of individuals actively sharing a common value or interest

concept.name: social networking
concept.definition: Social interactions, either in person or online, intended to share information and build relationships around common interests

concept.name: demographics
concept.definition: The study of human populations and their characteristics, for example statistics or trends around aging populations in a particular geographic region

concept.name: immigration
concept.definition: The movement of individuals or groups of people from one country to another

concept.name: emigration
concept.definition: Leaving one's country of residence to settle permanently elsewhere

concept.name: illegal immigration
concept.definition: The movement of individuals or groups of people from one country to another without legal authorisation from the destination country

concept.name: population and census
concept.definition: The official count of people living in a given geopolitical area, usually carried out by a governmental body

concept.name: discrimination
concept.definition: Unfair treatment of, or policies or practices against, individuals or groups of people on the basis of real or perceived membership in a group, such as race, sexual orientation, political or religious beliefs, age or height

concept.name: ageism
concept.definition: Discrimination against individuals or groups of people on the basis of age

concept.name: racism
concept.definition: Discrimination against individuals or groups of people on the basis of race

concept.name: religious discrimination
concept.definition: Unfair treatment of individuals or groups of people on the basis of their religious belief

concept.name: sexism
concept.definition: Discrimination against individuals or groups of people, usually women, on the basis of gender
concept.note: See also medtop:20001326 women's rights

concept.name: family
concept.definition: A group of people related genetically or by a legal bond, or who consider themselves part of a familial unit regardless of genetic or legal status

concept.name: adoption
concept.definition: The legal process of transferring parental rights to someone other than a person's birth parents, that person usually being a child

concept.name: Dating and Relationships
concept.definition: The development of an intimate connection between individuals, through various forms of activities enjoyed together, often leading to a legal or permanent union such as marriage

concept.name: divorce
concept.definition: The process by which a marriage is legally dissolved

concept.name: family planning
concept.definition: Services and education aimed at informing individual decisions about reproduction, such as fertility, in vitro fertilisation, contraception or abortion

concept.name: abortion
concept.definition: The intentional termination of a pregnancy for elective or medical reasons

concept.name: marriage
concept.definition: The legal or socially recognised union of individuals, which establishes rights and obligations between them

concept.name: parenting
concept.definition: The caring for and support of a child's physical, emotional, developmental and social needs from birth to adulthood

concept.name: demographic group
concept.definition: A subset of society with shared traits

concept.name: adults
concept.definition: People above an age set by law that entitles them to full liability and certain privileges such as the legal right to drink alcoholic beverages and to vote

concept.name: children
concept.definition: Young people, usually pre-pubescent, who are legally considered minors and are in a guardianship relationship with adults

concept.name: disabilities
concept.definition: Physical or mental conditions that limit a person's movements, senses, or activities

concept.name: LGBTQ
concept.definition: People who identify as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender or queer

concept.name: gender
concept.definition: The classification of individuals as male, female, or a non-binary designation

concept.name: infants
concept.definition: Very young people, usually defined as being just born up to the age of two

concept.name: national or ethnic minority
concept.definition: Groups of people that form a minority on an ethnic or national basis, and their status or issues relating to the majority

concept.name: nuclear radiation victims
concept.definition: Includes antinuclear movements and compensation for atomic blast victims.
concept.note: 2019-10-07: Retired. Use nuclear accident and incident (20000142) instead.

concept.name: senior citizens
concept.definition: People having passed the age of retirement

concept.name: teenagers
concept.definition: Young people between the ages that describe childhood and adulthood

concept.name: social condition
concept.definition: The circumstances or state of affairs affecting a person's life, welfare and relations with others in a society

concept.name: homelessness
concept.definition: The social condition defined by lack of permanent residence, living in shelters or on streets, and the issues and problems associated with it

concept.name: poverty
concept.definition: The lack of sufficient resources and means to provide basic needs such as food, clothing or shelter for oneself and one's family

concept.name: social problem
concept.definition: Issues related to human rights, human welfare and other areas of societal concern

concept.name: abusive behaviour
concept.definition: Actions that intentionally harm another person or people, often on an ongoing basis, such as psychological or mental abuse, negligence, physical or sexual abuse and torture

concept.name: addiction
concept.definition: The habitual and compulsive use of substances such as alcohol or drugs, or behaviour such as gambling, gaming or sex, often causing detrimental effects on the body, brain, and relationships with others

concept.name: juvenile delinquency
concept.definition: Unlawful conduct perpetrated by minors, often on a repeated basis

concept.name: prostitution
concept.definition: The business of engaging in sexual activity in exchange for payment

concept.name: slavery
concept.definition: The ownership of people as property, and the involuntary servitude of those people to their owners, which includes unpaid labour and coerced actions

concept.name: values
concept.definition: A person's or group's principles or standards of behaviour, which guide their way of living and choices made

concept.name: corrupt practices
concept.definition: Any action which is harmful to others
concept.note: 2019-10-07: Retired. Use corruption (20000093) instead.

concept.name: death and dying
concept.definition: Social, medical and mental health issues relating to people at the end of their lives

concept.name: euthanasia
concept.definition: The practice of humanely ending the life of a person suffering from a terminal illness

concept.name: assisted suicide
concept.definition: The practice of assisting a person suffering from a terminal illness in the process of suicide, often with a physician's oversight

concept.name: suicide
concept.definition: The intentional taking of one's own life

concept.name: ethics
concept.definition: The moral values and standards that define right and wrong actions or decisions

concept.name: pornography
concept.definition: The depiction of sexually explicit acts in various media renditions, such as video or photos, often considered obscene or immoral

concept.name: sexual behaviour
concept.definition: The manner in which people express their sexuality in physical acts

concept.name: welfare
concept.definition: Help for those in need of food, housing, health and other services

concept.name: charity
concept.definition: The voluntary giving of money, food or other necessities to those in need

concept.name: long-term care
concept.definition: Services provided on an extended and ongoing basis to patients suffering from chronic illness or disability

concept.name: social services
concept.definition: Social programmes, usually publicly sponsored, aimed at promoting people's welfare, such as housing, health care or education services

concept.name: competition discipline
concept.definition: Different types of sport which can be executed in competitions

concept.name: American football
concept.definition: Team ball game that opposes two teams of 11 that have offence and defence sections. Each offence attempts to move an oval ball down the 120 yards long field. Points are scored by advancing the ball into the opposing team’s end zone.
concept.note: 2016-04-06: definition rephrased.

concept.name: archery
concept.definition: Archers use bows and arrows to aim at targets 1.22 metres in diameter which are on average 70 metres distant.

concept.name: crossbow shooting
concept.definition: Shooting with crossbow on targets from different distances

concept.name: longbow
concept.definition: Shooting with longbow on targets from different distances. The competition is held outdoors on an open (flat) field.

concept.name: athletics
concept.definition: Competitions involving foot races, jumping and throwing which can be on a track inside a stadium or on outside roads

concept.name: cross-country run
concept.definition: Competitors run over rough ground and not on a track

concept.name: decathlon
concept.definition: Individual men's competition which involves accumulating points in 10 different disciplines over two days: 1st day - 100m, long-jump, shot-putt, high-jump, 400m. 2nd day - 110m hurdles, discus, pole-vault, javelin and 1,500 metres.

concept.name: discus throw
concept.definition: Competitors stand in a netted circle with a 2.50 metres diameter and with a turning motion attempt to throw a discus weighing 2kg for men and 1kg for women as far as possible

concept.name: hammer throw
concept.definition: Competitors throw a hammer consisting of a metal ball, chain and handle as far as they can from a netted circle which is 2.13 metres in diameter. The hammer weighs 7.26kg for men and 4kg for women

concept.name: heptathlon
concept.definition: Competition involving seven separate disciplines over two days. 1st day - 100m hurdles, high jump, shot put, 200 metres. 2nd day - long-jump, javelin, 800 metres

concept.name: high jump
concept.definition: Competitors build up speed over a short sprint and then jump as high as possible over a horizontal bar

concept.name: hurdles
concept.definition: A sprint running race where athletes jump over a series of upright frames

concept.name: javelin throw
concept.definition: Competitors in a specially-designated throwing area attempt to fling a spear-like javelin as far as possible. The javelin weighs 800 grams for men and 600 grams for women.

concept.name: long distance run
concept.definition: A running race at distances for more than 1500 metres

concept.name: long jump
concept.definition: Competitors build up speed over a short sprint and then jump in one leap as far as possible

concept.name: middle distance run
concept.definition: A running race at distances for more than 400 metres and up to and including 1500 metres

concept.name: pentathlon
concept.definition: Only indoor, 60m hurdles, high jump, shot put, long jump, 800 m

concept.name: pole vault
concept.definition: Competitors use a flexible pole to propel themselves feet-first into the air and clear a bar which is raised higher at each round of jumps.

concept.name: race walking
concept.definition: A race requiring the competitor to have always one foot on the ground

concept.name: relay run
concept.definition: A team of athletes run a relay over identical distances

concept.name: shot put
concept.definition: Competitors stand in a circle with a 2.13 metres diameter and attempt to throw a metal ball weighing 7.26 kg for men and 4kg for women as far as possible. The longest throw wins.

concept.name: sprint run
concept.definition: A running race at distances of up to and including 400 metres

concept.name: triple jump
concept.definition: Competitors build up speed over a short sprint and then attempt to jump as far forward as they can using a hop, skip and a jump technique.

concept.name: Australian rules football
concept.definition: A form of football played by two teams of 18 competing on a pitch by running with and passing an oval ball. Points are scored by moving the ball between goal posts. Governed by the Australian Football League.
concept.note: 2016-04-06: definition rephrased.

concept.name: badminton
concept.definition: Two players or two teams of two compete by hitting a shuttlecock weighing approximately five grams over a high net. The aim for each player/team is to prevent the shuttlecock landing on their side of the court.

concept.name: bandy
concept.definition: Played outdoors on ice. The size of the ice rink is about the size of a soccer field. Skates like ice hockey. A small hard ball is used for playing. Players play with sticks, much like in ice hockey, but the sticks are shorter and more rounded than in ice

concept.name: baseball
concept.definition: A game between two teams of nine played on an enclosed ground. The team which scores the most points wins. A point is scored when a player runs around the ground marked out by four bases. To do this he has to hit the ball thrown at him by a rival player.

concept.name: rubberball baseball
concept.definition: Sometimes called ''soft baseball,'' the game is played with a rubber baseball of varying degrees of hardness depending upon the age and level of the players.

concept.name: basketball
concept.definition: Game played between two teams of five - points are scored by placing the large inflated ball into a net fixed on a ring 3.05m above the ground.

concept.name: biathlon
concept.definition: A combination of cross-country skiing and target shooting on a 12.5 K course in a pursuit format.

concept.name: billiards
concept.definition: A cue sport with balls on a table without a specific order to play them

concept.name: bobsleigh
concept.definition: One, two or four people racing down a course in a sled that consists of a main hull, a frame, two axles and sets of runners. The total time of all heats in a competition is added together to determine the winner.

concept.name: boules
concept.definition: Collective name of a number of games where the common goal is to move the competitor's balls as close to a small target ball as possible, played on a grass or sand surface. Balls are thrown (as in petanque and boules) or rolled (as in lawn bowls or bocce).

concept.name: boxing
concept.definition: Combat sport in which two men/women fight using only their fists covered by padded gloves in a square ring measuring 6m a side. The fight is usually split into 12 rounds of three minutes each.

concept.name: bantamweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: cruiserweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: featherweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: flyweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: heavyweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: light flyweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: light-heavyweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: light-middleweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: light-welterweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: lightweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: middleweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: straw weight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: super-bantamweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: super-featherweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: super-flyweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: super-heavyweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: super-middleweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: welterweight (boxing)
concept.definition: Weight category which varies according to the federation or the Olympic Games

concept.name: bullfighting
concept.definition: Classical contest pitting man against the bull

concept.name: Canadian football
concept.definition: Team ball game that opposes two teams of 12 that have offence and defence sections. Each offence attempts to move an oval ball down the 150 yards long field. Points are scored by advancing the ball into the opposing team’s end zone.
concept.note: 2016-04-06: definition rephrased.

concept.name: canoeing
concept.definition: Competition involving canoes which are generally kneeled and paddled with a single-bladed paddle

concept.name: C1 (canoeing)
concept.definition: One-seater canoe (open boat with competitor using a single bladed paddle

concept.name: C2 (canoeing)
concept.definition: Two-seater canoe (open boat) - competitors use a single bladed paddle

concept.name: C4 (canoeing)
concept.definition: Four-seater canoe (open boat) with each competitor using a single bladed paddle

concept.name: canoe sailing
concept.definition: A race involving a canoe equipped with a sail.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic canoeing + sports facet canoekayaktype

concept.name: pontoniering
concept.definition: Team sport, military tradition, similar to canoe regatta, in boats
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic canoeing + sports facet canoekayaktype

concept.name: casting (fishing)
concept.definition: Using fishing equipment to hit a target and score points

concept.name: mountain climbing
concept.definition: Moving up mountains using hands and feet

concept.name: ice climbing
concept.definition: Climbing of ice features such as icefalls and frozen cliffs

concept.name: mountaineering
concept.definition: Competitive alpine climbing

concept.name: sport climbing
concept.definition: Sport climbing as a special category: individual competition, on an artificial wall with time measurement

concept.name: cricket
concept.definition: Ball sport involving two teams of 11 players, balls, bats and wickets. The aim is to score as many runs as possible, and to get the opposing team 'out'. A 'run' involves a player running between two wickets. The opposing team try to get their rivals 'out'

concept.name: croquet
concept.definition: Using mallets to hit a ball through hoops

concept.name: curling
concept.definition: A game played on ice with large flat round stones. A player throws the stone, aiming at a target. Teammates of the player who has thrown the stone can sweep the ice in front of the stone to help smooth its path towards the target.

concept.name: icestock sport
concept.definition: Similar to curling, played on targets on ice, different stones with straight handle and different rules, played in alpine countries

concept.name: cycling
concept.definition: A race over a given distance on bicycles.

concept.name: artistic cycling
concept.definition: Individual or teams on one or two bicycles in an arena. Points are awarded by a jury.

concept.name: bi-crossing
concept.definition: A race where competitors have to cover a circuit with obstacles as quickly as possible.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facet cyclingtype

concept.name: bmx racing
concept.definition: Bicycle racing in with inline start and various obstacles over off-road circuits.

concept.name: cycle ball
concept.definition: Competitive team sport using bicycles. One team against another with two persons per team. The aim is for competitors to move the ball using the front and rear wheels without letting their feet touch the ground.

concept.name: cycle sprint race
concept.definition: Track cycling race between two cyclists over two or three laps, with a timed sprint finish over the last 200m
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facet racetype

concept.name: cyclo-cross
concept.definition: Staged on various surfaces - earth, grass, sand or tarmac.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facet cyclingtype

concept.name: individual pursuit (cycling)
concept.definition: Track cycling event where two cyclists set off from midway down opposite straights and try to make up the gap between them
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling +sports facets racetype + teamsize

concept.name: individual time trial (cycling)
concept.definition: A race or stage against the clock with each cyclist setting off individually
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facets racetype + teamsize

concept.name: Keirin
concept.definition: Sprint between six cyclists where a pacemaker on a moped gradually increases the pace over the first 1400m. When the pacemaker leaves the track the race develops into a sprint finish over the final 6-700m.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facet cyclingtype

concept.name: Madison race
concept.definition: A points race by teams comprising two cyclists in relay-format
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facet cyclingtype

concept.name: mountain biking
concept.definition: All-terrain, off-road bicycle riding requiring endurance and specially adapted mountain bikes

concept.name: points race (cycling)
concept.definition: Cyclists aim to complete the highest number of laps. Bonus points are picked up in intermediate sprints held every 2500m
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facet cyclingtype

concept.name: road cycling
concept.definition: Road race with all competitors starting en masse.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facet cyclingtype

concept.name: cycling staging race
concept.definition: Race disputed by teams with the classifications determined by the combined times over a day
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facet racetype

concept.name: team cycle sprint
concept.definition: A track race where two teams race against each other, starting on opposite sides of the track, following the first lap the first rider pulls away, leaving the second rider to lead the next lap, who then does the same at the last lap, leaving the third rider to finish the race. The team with the fastest time is the winner.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facet cyclingtype

concept.name: road cycling team pursuit
concept.definition: Track cycle event where two teams set off from midway down opposite straights and try to make up the gap between them
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facets racetype + teamsize.

concept.name: team time trial (cycling)
concept.definition: Teams of cyclists race against the clock
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facets racetype + teamsize.

concept.name: track race (cycling)
concept.definition: A race staged on a track in a velodrome
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic cycling + sports facet cyclingtype

concept.name: competitive dancing
concept.definition: Dancing competition where competitors are judged on technique and interpretation.

concept.name: darts
concept.definition: The sport in which small darts are thrown at a dartboard, a circular target divided into numbered sections.

concept.name: diving
concept.definition: Competitors dive off a fixed or spring board and are assessed by seven judges giving marks up to ten for their acrobatic moves.

concept.name: platform diving
concept.definition: Divers take off from a high platform

concept.name: springboard diving
concept.definition: Divers dive from a high springboard.

concept.name: synchronised diving
concept.definition: Two competitors from the same team dive off at the same time from separate platforms or springboards. As well as being judged on the standard criteria they are also awarded marks for synchronisation.

concept.name: scuba diving
concept.definition: Underwater diving with the use of a self-contained underwater breathing apparatus

concept.name: underwater sports
concept.definition: Competitive sports played under water

concept.name: dog racing
concept.definition: Dogs racing around a track

concept.name: oval track dog racing
concept.definition: Pursuing an artificial rabbit around an oval track
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic dog racing+ sports facet dogracingtype

concept.name: dog sled racing
concept.definition: Race with dogs harnessed to a sled
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic dog racing+ sports facet dogracingtype

concept.name: duathlon
concept.definition: An athletic event involving running and cycling

concept.name: equestrian
concept.definition: Horse-based sport disciplines governed by the International Federation for Equestrian Sports, such as dressage, eventing, show jumping and vaulting

concept.name: cross-country horse riding
concept.definition: Equestrian event held on a designated circuit in the countryside featuring artificial and natural obstacles.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic equestrianism + sports facet equestriantype.

concept.name: dressage
concept.definition: A rider is judged on a series of moves at three different paces - walk, trot and gallop.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic equestrianism + sports facet equestriantype.

concept.name: horse jumping
concept.definition: A rider and horse attempt to jump a series of obstacles in an enclosed arena against the clock.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic equestrianism + sports facet equestriantype.

concept.name: three day eventing
concept.definition: Equestrian event involving three disciplines - Dressage, cross-country and show jumping
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic equestrianism + sports facet equestriantype.

concept.name: horse driving
concept.definition: Equestrian events involving horses being driven in harness, single vehicle around a course.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic equestrianism + sports facet equestriantype.

concept.name: fencing
concept.definition: Combat sport using a sword or foil.

concept.name: epee
concept.definition: Category of fencing using a weapon with a triangular cross-section blade and a large bell guard
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic fencing + sports facet fencingtype.

concept.name: foil
concept.definition: Category of fencing using a weapon with a rectangular cross-section blade and a small bell guard
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic fencing + sports facet fencingtype.

concept.name: sabre
concept.definition: A fencing weapon with a flat blade and knuckle guard used with cutting or thrusting moves
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic fencing + sports facet fencingtype.

concept.name: field hockey
concept.definition: A ball sport involving two teams of 11 players using curved sticks. The aim is to score as many goals as possible.

concept.name: figure skating
concept.definition: To obtain the best marks possible from nine judges who award scores after two prepared sections - both skated to music - during which competitors must attempt to achieve the greatest possible harmony between artistic flair and technical precision.

concept.name: ice dance
concept.definition: Takes place over three days with, in order, two compulsory dances, the original dance (2min 30sec) and the free dance (4min).
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic figure skating + sports facet figureskatetype.

concept.name: fist ball
concept.definition: Men's team sport similar to volleyball (with a much lower net) executed mainly in gymnastics clubs

concept.name: floorball
concept.definition: Played indoors in a court the size of a basketball court. 6 players per team, of which one is a goalkeeper. He/she operates kneeling but there is no goal-stick like in ice hockey. The players use plastic clubs and a light plastic ball to pass and shot goal

concept.name: flying disc
concept.definition: A group of events all played with a flying plastic disc. The exact regulation for the disc is different in different events but it has to be unbroken of solid plastic and a production model with no modification. Flying disc is sometimes called Frisbee.

concept.name: Gaelic football
concept.definition: A form of football played by two teams of 15 competing on a pitch by running with, kicking and passing a spherical ball. Points are scored by moving the ball between goal posts. Governed by the Gaelic Athletic Association.
concept.note: 2016-04-06: definition rephrased.

concept.name: golf
concept.definition: A game to hit a small hard ball with different clubs around a course of typically 18 holes varying in distance and during a round. The object, depending on the scoring formulae, is to make the fewest strokes possible

concept.name: mini golf
concept.definition: A game where small hard balls must be holed on an artificial course in as few strokes from the club as possible

concept.name: gymnastics
concept.definition: A sport consisting of a variety of disciplines in which gymnasts perform artistic and acrobatic moves using different apparatus.

concept.name: ball (rhythmic gymnastics)
concept.definition: An exercise based on suppleness of the body using a ball.

concept.name: beam (artistic gymnastics)
concept.definition: Gymnasts perform leaps and flips from the much feared balance beam just 10 cm wide.

concept.name: clubs (rhythmic gymnastics)
concept.definition: Dance and gymnastic moves whilst juggling clubs.

concept.name: floor exercise (artistic gymnastics)
concept.definition: A gymnastics floor exercise on a 12m/sq matt.

concept.name: hoop (rhythmic gymnastics)
concept.definition: A hoop is rolled and thrown whilst performing a dance routine.

concept.name: horizontal bar (artistic gymnastics)
concept.definition: Gymnasts turn, spin and perform pirouettes around the horizontal bar.

concept.name: parallel bars (artistic gymnastics)
concept.definition: Gymnasts swing and turn with guile and grace around the parallel bars.

concept.name: pommel horse (artistic gymnastics)
concept.definition: Gymnasts perform an uninterrupted swing motion over a handled horse using balance and strength

concept.name: rhythmic gymnastics
concept.definition: Mixture of dance and gymnastic moves

concept.name: ribbon (rhythmic gymnastics)
concept.definition: Gymnasts work through a pre-arranged routine whilst manipulating a spectacular length of ribbon.

concept.name: rings (artistic gymnastics)
concept.definition: Gymnasts perform feats of balance and strength from two suspended rings.

concept.name: rope (rhythmic gymnastics)
concept.definition: Jump and figure movements are performed while a rope is manipulated.

concept.name: trampoline
concept.definition: Competitors perform complicated twists and turns whilst bouncing on the trampoline.

concept.name: uneven bars (artistic gymnastics)
concept.definition: Gymnasts run through an airborne routine swinging around and between the uneven bars.

concept.name: vault (artistic gymnastics)
concept.definition: Gymnasts propel themselves by jumping on a trampoline after running quickly down a 25m lane

concept.name: handball (team)
concept.definition: A ball game using the hands contested by two teams of seven trying to throw the ball into the opponents goal.

concept.name: hornuss
concept.definition: Swiss team sport with 16 or 18 men per team. A rubber puck is hit towards the field of the adversary team which tries to hit the puck in the air with a wooden board thrown in the air

concept.name: horse racing
concept.definition: Mounted horse races.

concept.name: flat racing
concept.definition: A horse race over a flat course.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic horse racing + sports facet equestriantype.

concept.name: harness racing
concept.definition: Horses hitched to a jockeyed buggy race round a flat course. Can be performed at a trotting gait or a pacing gait.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic horse racing + sports facet equestriantype.

concept.name: steeple chase
concept.definition: A mounted horse race over at least eight classic obstacles, four of which must be different, such as the water jump, spruce fences, mud walls etc.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic horse racing + sports facet equestriantype.

concept.name: hurling
concept.definition: A game played by two teams of fifteen each with a curved wooden stick called a hurley. The object is to score a goal by hitting the small ball between the opponents' goalposts either over the crossbar or under it into a net guarded by a goalkeeper.

concept.name: ice hockey
concept.definition: Two teams of six heavily padded skaters try and outscore each other by hitting a puck into the opponents goal.

concept.name: sledge hockey
concept.definition: Like ice hockey but instead of skates small sledges are used. Competitors move by pushing with the arms

concept.name: inline skating
concept.definition: Competitive sports using inline skates
concept.note: Retired. Use roller sports (medtop:20001155)

concept.name: Jai Alai (Pelota)
concept.definition: A fast ball play using either the bare hand or a wicker scoop in an arena of walls of different outlay and size

concept.name: judo
concept.definition: A defensive martial art.
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: broader term changed

concept.name: extra lightweight (judo)
concept.definition: Up to 60kg for men and 48kg for women.

concept.name: half-heavyweight (judo)
concept.definition: Up to 100kg for men and 78kg for women.

concept.name: half-lightweight (judo)
concept.definition: Up to 73kg for men and 57kg for women.

concept.name: half-middleweight (judo)
concept.definition: Up to 81kg for men and 63kg for women

concept.name: heavyweight (judo)
concept.definition: Open but usually over 100kg for men and over 78kg for women

concept.name: lightweight (judo)
concept.definition: Up to 66kg for men and 52kg for women.

concept.name: middleweight (judo)
concept.definition: Up to 90kg for men and 70kg for women.

concept.name: jukendo
concept.definition: Japanese traditional martial art using a model rifle made of wood. 'Juken' means a rifle or gun with blade (bayonet)
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: broader term changed

concept.name: kabaddi
concept.definition: The attacking side scores by touching, and the side to guard scores by capturing. The attacker continues calling it 'kabaddi'.

concept.name: kayaking
concept.definition: Competition involving kayaks where the paddler sits with their legs facing forward using a double-bladed paddle

concept.name: K1 (kayaking)
concept.definition: An enclosed boat with the competitor sitting on a fixed seat for one person using a double bladed paddle

concept.name: K2 (kayaking)
concept.definition: An enclosed boat with the competitor sitting on a fixed seat for two people using double bladed paddles

concept.name: K4 (kayaking)
concept.definition: An enclosed boat with the competitor sitting on a fixed seat for four people using double bladed paddles

concept.name: karate
concept.definition: A martial art where chops, punches, kicks and throws are used to defeat an opponent.
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: broader term changed

concept.name: kendo
concept.definition: Japanese traditional martial art using a bamboo sword, sometimes called Japanese fencing
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: broader term changed

concept.name: kyudo
concept.definition: Japanese traditional martial art using a bamboo bow and arrows, sometimes called Japanese archery
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: broader term changed

concept.name: lacrosse
concept.definition: Two teams of helmeted and padded players try and outscore each other using a netted stick and hard ball.

concept.name: luge
concept.definition: Luge (French word for sled) is competed in singles or doubles. The competitor(s) lay on their back on an open sled and race down a course. The competitor(s) and the sled must be in contact when passing the finishing line. The competitor can steer the sled

concept.name: marathon
concept.definition: A road race where competitors run 42.195km, generally through city streets

concept.name: modern pentathlon
concept.definition: The Modern Pentathlon comprises five events run over a single day in the following order: shooting, fencing, swimming, horse riding and running.

concept.name: motorboat racing
concept.definition: Racing with boats with engines

concept.name: motor car racing
concept.definition: Racing with cars

concept.name: endurance race
concept.definition: Races for one or more drivers over a long and set distance or time
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motor car racing + sports facet motorcarracingtype

concept.name: F3000
concept.definition: Using less powerful motors than the Formula One, designated construction teams race over varied circuits the world over in speed races for individual and team honours over a season.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motor car racing + sports facet motorcarracingtype

concept.name: Formula One
