concept.definition: Single cockpit car racing where 20 or so competitors race head to head over varied circuits around the world to win Grand Prix points in an individual and constructors championship
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motor car racing + sports facet motorcarracingtype

concept.name: Indy Racing
concept.definition: North American top range professional league speed racing over circuits.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motor car racing + sports facet motorcarracingtype

concept.name: motor car rallycross
concept.definition: Stage races over dirt tracks
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motor car racing + sports facet motorcarracingtype

concept.name: motor car rallying
concept.definition: A season of endurance races over closed circuits including dirt tracks, roads and even snowy conditions featuring specials using adapted road cars with a driver and co-pilot.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motor car racing + sports facet motorcarracingtype

concept.name: motorcycling
concept.definition: Races with 2, 3 or 4 wheels vehicles with a saddle and handlebars

concept.name: motorcycle endurance
concept.definition: A road race of consistency and endurance
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topicmotorcycling+ sports facet motorcyclingtype.

concept.name: enduro
concept.definition: A race of speed, endurance and regularity over varied surfaces such as through forests and on dirt tracks
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motorcycling+ sports facet motorcyclingtype

concept.name: grass-track
concept.definition: A race competed on turf
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topicmotorcycling+ sports facet motorcyclingtype

concept.name: moto-ball
concept.definition: Team sport where drivers play a ball
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motorcycling+ sports facet racetype.

concept.name: moto-cross
concept.definition: Raced over dirt tracks featuring severe mounds
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motorcycling+ sports facet racetype.

concept.name: motoGP
concept.definition: Capacity class 1000ccm
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motorcycling+ sports facet racetype.

concept.name: motorcycle rallying
concept.definition: Races run over regular roads
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topicmotorcycling+ sports facet motorcyclingtype

concept.name: side-cars
concept.definition: Of different engine sizes but attached to a side car featuring a passenger
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motorcycling+ sports facet racetype.

concept.name: speedway
concept.definition: Race involving up to six riders competing over a flat oval track. Speedway motorcycles use only one gear and have no brakes
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topicmotorcycling+ sports facet motorcyclingtype

concept.name: motorcycling trial
concept.definition: A race of consistency and endurance over varied terrain
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic motorcycling+ sports facet racetype.

concept.name: naginata
concept.definition: Japanese traditional martial art using a pole sword made of wood. 'Naginata' means a spear with a curved blade
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: broader term changed

concept.name: netball
concept.definition: A woman's sport similar to basketball though without a board behind the basket

concept.name: orienteering
concept.definition: An individual time-trial over a route marked out by beacons. The competitor has to search out and find in a specific order. The contestant makes his way with the help of a compass and map

concept.name: ski orienteering
concept.definition: An individual ski time-trial over a route marked out by beacons, that the competitor has to search out and find in a specific order. The contestant makes his way with the help of a compass and map

concept.name: parachuting
concept.definition: Jumping from an aeroplane using a parachute, competitions are precision landings, voltige, individual and team

concept.name: polo
concept.definition: With the aid of a mallet two teams of four horsemen try and knock a bamboo ball into the opponents goal over a pitch 250m long and 150m wide. The game is divided into 4, 6, or 8 time periods of 7min 30sec called chukkas.

concept.name: pool
concept.definition: A cue sport on a table with pockets, played with different numbers of balls

concept.name: power boating
concept.definition: Races between motor boats on rivers or lakes

concept.name: rodeo
concept.definition: A discipline where wild horses or bulls must be mounted and mastered

concept.name: bareback bronc
concept.definition: Rodeo discipline

concept.name: barrel racing
concept.definition: Rodeo discipline

concept.name: bull riding
concept.definition: Rodeo discipline

concept.name: bulldogging
concept.definition: Rodeo discipline

concept.name: calf roping
concept.definition: Rodeo discipline

concept.name: goat roping
concept.definition: Rodeo discipline

concept.name: saddle bronc
concept.definition: Rodeo discipline

concept.name: roller hockey
concept.definition: Team sport similar to ice hockey, executed on 4 wheel roller skates with sticks and a ball

concept.name: rowing
concept.definition: Boat racing usually on flat calm waters with boats for 1, 2, 4 or 8 rowers

concept.name: coxed four
concept.definition: Four person boats with just one oar each and with a team mate to give directions

concept.name: coxed pair
concept.definition: Two person boats with just one oar each and with a team mate to give directions

concept.name: coxless four
concept.definition: Four person boats with just one oar each and without a team mate to give directions

concept.name: coxless pair
concept.definition: Two person boats with just one oar each and without a team mate to give directions

concept.name: double sculls
concept.definition: Two person boats with two oars each

concept.name: coxed eight
concept.definition: Eight person boats with just one oar each and with a team mate to give directions

concept.name: quadruple sculls
concept.definition: Four person boats with two oars each and without a team mate to give directions

concept.name: single sculls
concept.definition: Single person boats with two oars

concept.name: rugby league
concept.definition: A type of rugby where two teams of 13 compete on a pitch by running with and passing an oval ball. Points are scored by touching down the ball behind the goal line or kicking it between goal posts. Governed by Rugby League International Federation.
concept.note: 2016-04-05: Broader concept changed, definition slightly modified.

concept.name: rugby union
concept.definition: A type of rugby where two teams of 15 compete on a pitch by running with and passing an oval ball. Points are scored by touching down the ball behind the goal line or kicking it between goal posts. Governed by the World Rugby organization.
concept.note: 2016-04-05: Broader concept changed, definition slightly modified.

concept.name: rugby sevens
concept.definition: A type of rugby union where two teams of 7 compete on a pitch by running with and passing an oval ball. Points are scored by touching down the ball behind the goal line or kicking it between goal posts. Governed by the World Rugby organization.
concept.note: 2016-04-05: Broader concept changed, definition slightly modified.

concept.name: sailing
concept.definition: Sailing boat racing over a route

concept.name: multihull
concept.definition: Sailing boats with multiple hulls
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sailing + sports facet sailingtype.

concept.name: dinghy
concept.definition: Single hulled vessel without a fixed keel
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sailing + sports facet sailingtype.

concept.name: keelboat
concept.definition: Single hulled vessel with a fixed keel
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sailing + sports facet sailingtype.

concept.name: sailing regatta
concept.definition: Sailing event
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sailing + sports facet sailingtype.

concept.name: ocean sailing
concept.definition: Sailing vessels across oceans as part of a competitive race.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sailing + sports facet sailingtype.

concept.name: round the world race
concept.definition: Sailing vessels around the world as a race
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sailing + sports facet sailingtype.

concept.name: solo ocean sailing
concept.definition: Sailing oceans as an individual, as part of a race.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sailing + sports facet sailingtype.

concept.name: non-stop solo race
concept.definition: Races either clockwise or anti clockwise around the globe without stopping at any ports between start and finish of race.
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sailing + sports facet sailingtype.

concept.name: sepak takraw
concept.definition: A game like volleyball where hands must not be used, using a ball made of rattan

concept.name: shinty
concept.definition: A ball game involving 2 teams with curved sticks called camans. The object is to score goals by hitting the small leather ball through the goals.

concept.name: sport shooting
concept.definition: Precision sport using a hand gun, rifle or shotgun

concept.name: pistol shooting
concept.definition: Shooting event using a pistol
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sport shooting + sports facet shootingtype.

concept.name: rifle shooting
concept.definition: Shooting event using a rifle
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sport shooting + sports facet shootingtype.

concept.name: running target shooting
concept.definition: Shooting event with an airgun and a target that moves sideways
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sport shooting + sports facet shootingtype.

concept.name: shotgun shooting
concept.definition: Shooting event using a shotgun
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sport shooting + sports facet shootingtype.

concept.name: clay pigeon shooting
concept.definition: Shooting event which involves shooting at special flying targets, known as clay pigeons or clay targets
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic sport shooting + sports facet shootingtype.

concept.name: skeleton
concept.definition: In skeleton the competitor lies on his/her stomach when racing down the course. The competitor must be on the sled when crossing the finishing line.

concept.name: skiing
concept.definition: A winter sport using different types of skis

concept.name: alpine skiing
concept.definition: Racing downhill on snow-covered slopes using alpine techniques and equipment. The slopes contain intermediate gates which are spaced according to the discipline involved.

concept.name: freestyle skiing
concept.definition: Skiing competitions which, in contrast to alpine skiing, incorporate acrobatic moves and jumps. Events include aerials, halfpipe, slopestyle, ski cross, moguls and big air.

concept.name: grass skiing
concept.definition: Alpine skiing on roller skis and grass

concept.name: Nordic skiing
concept.definition: Practiced in relatively-flat, snowy countryside and based on power and endurance.

concept.name: ski jumping
concept.definition: Competitors descend a snow covered elevated ramp on skis and try to fly off it as far as possible, receiving marks for distance, style of flight and landing

concept.name: sky diving
concept.definition: Parachuting competitions with long distance falls without using a parachute

concept.name: snooker
concept.definition: A cue sport with balls on a table to be potted in a specific order

concept.name: snowboarding
concept.definition: Practiced with a single board (rather than two skis)

concept.name: football
concept.definition: Also known as soccer. Played by two teams competing on a pitch by kicking a spherical ball. Points are scored by kicking or heading the ball into a goal. Governed by the Fédération Internationale de Football Association
concept.note: 2016-04-06: definition rephrased.

concept.name: softball
concept.definition: Similar to baseball but with a larger and softer ball, a thinner bat, shorter gaps between bases and less innings

concept.name: speed skating
concept.definition: Timed races competed by two or more skaters at a time on an oval ice track

concept.name: squash
concept.definition: A racquet sport of strategy and endurance played by singles or doubles teams in a walled court with a small rubber ball

concept.name: sumo wrestling
concept.definition: A combat sport of speed and rapidity with much ceremony where two contestants try to throw or slap a competitor out of a marked area. Generally practiced in Japan by large men.

concept.name: surfing
concept.definition: Water sport where contestants catch and ride waves upright on a surfboard

concept.name: swimming
concept.definition: A water sport where contestants swim as fast as possible in a given style and win races by being the first to touch home

concept.name: backstroke
concept.definition: Swimming style, on the back, legs move in flapping motion whilst arms propel the swimmer forward

concept.name: breaststroke
concept.definition: Swimming style , on the front, effecting forward and outward push movements with the arms and backward and outward pushes with the legs

concept.name: butterfly swimming
concept.definition: Swimming style , swum on the front with the shoulders remaining parallel to the water both arms moving in simultaneous windmill fashion, the legs remain together and are moved in a flapping motion. The most physical of all styles.

concept.name: freestyle swimming
concept.definition: Any swimming style may be employed, though front crawl is universally employed

concept.name: medley swimming
concept.definition: A combination of four different swimming styles in one race

concept.name: marathon swimming
concept.definition: A long distance open water swimming
concept.note: Retired in 2017 in favour of sports facet, but then un-retired in 2021 so all top-level Olympics sports have a valid Media Topics term.

concept.name: relay freestyle swimming
concept.definition: Team of swimmers swim a relay in the swimming style of their choice

concept.name: relay medley swimming
concept.definition: Team relay race involving a combination of four different swimming styles

concept.name: short course swimming
concept.definition: Races competed in a 25m length pool

concept.name: synchronised free routine
concept.definition: In the free programme, music, movements and their order of execution are not restricted.
concept.note: 2016-04-05: Broader concept changed.

concept.name: synchronised technical routine
concept.definition: Contestants obtain points from 10 sitting judges by executing certain synchronised artistic shapes and movements in the water. In the technical programme, competitors must execute a range of specific moves in a given order.
concept.note: 2016-04-05: Broader concept changed

concept.name: table tennis
concept.definition: A racquet sport for two or four (in doubles), who compete at a table divided by a net using a small bat to play a lightweight ball

concept.name: Taekwon-Do
concept.definition: A martial art of Korean origin
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: broader term changed

concept.name: tennis
concept.definition: A sport where two players, four in doubles, equipped with a racket compete by hitting the ball over a net into the opponent's side of the court with the aim of putting it out of reach within the regulation lines, thus winning points.

concept.name: ten pin bowling
concept.definition: A game in which a player scores points by trying to knock down ten 'pins' using a ball - if unsuccessful the player has a second attempt. A game consists of ten frames.

concept.name: triathlon
concept.definition: An endurance multi-sport where competitors first swim, then cycle and then run a road race. Distances vary according to the competition.

concept.name: tug-of-war
concept.definition: Two teams pulling against each other on a rope

concept.name: volleyball
concept.definition: Two teams of six record points by hitting a ball over a net into the opponent's half of the court, keeping it in the air at all times. Points are won when opponents fail to return the ball.

concept.name: beach volleyball
concept.definition: Two teams of two players compete on a sand court. Unlike indoor volleyball, points are scored only when the serving team wins a rally or forces an error. The sand surface makes it more physically demanding than volleyball

concept.name: water polo
concept.definition: Played in a pool between two teams of 7, who must stay afloat and can only use one hand to pass the ball or swim with it before trying to throw it into the opponents net to score goals.

concept.name: water skiing
concept.definition: On one or two skis contestants are pulled by a power boat along the water's surface

concept.name: weightlifting and powerlifting
concept.definition: A strength test where competitors lift as heavy a weight as possible

concept.name: clean and jerk (weightlifting)
concept.definition: The bar is first lifted to the shoulders and then jerk it above the head and keep it there while maintaining straight legs
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic weightlifting+ sports facet weightliftingtype.

concept.name: powerlifting
concept.definition: A strength test where competitors lift as heavy a weight as possible
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topic weightlifting+ sports facet weightliftingtype.

concept.name: snatch (weightlifting)
concept.definition: The bar must be lifted from floor to above the head in one movement
concept.note: Retired. Use Media Topicweightlifting+ sports facet weightliftingtype.

concept.name: windsurfing
concept.definition: Contestants must complete a marked out course as fast as possible on a windsurf board

concept.name: wrestling
concept.definition: Combat sport where each wrestler attempts to win over his adversary, by holding both shoulders on the ground (fall) long enough to be in control. Points can also be decisive.

concept.name: freestyle wrestling
concept.definition: A style where leg holds are allowed

concept.name: greco-roman
concept.definition: A style where the only holds allowed are those between the head and the belt

concept.name: Swiss wrestling
concept.definition: Traditional Swiss sport similar to wrestling with specific rules

concept.name: wushu
concept.definition: Chinese traditional martial art
concept.note: Change Note 2015-08-27: broader term changed

concept.name: disciplinary action in sport
concept.definition: Actions, including fines and suspensions levied by sports organisations and teams

concept.name: drug use in sport
concept.definition: Drug use associated with sport activities, including doping, abuse, testing and permitted medical uses

concept.name: drug abuse in sport
concept.definition: Using drugs to improve competitiveness in sports
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001104: drug use in sport instead

concept.name: drug testing in sport
concept.definition: Means and ways to check the use of drugs in sports
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001104: drug use in sport instead

concept.name: medical drug use in sport
concept.definition: Use of drugs for medical reasons as permitted by sport regulators
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001104: drug use in sport instead

concept.name: sport event
concept.definition: An event featuring one or more sport competitions

concept.name: continental championship
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from countries from one continent may participate. Called a "Championship"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: continental cup
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from countries from one continent may participate. Called a "Cup"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: continental games
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from countries from one continent may participate. Called "Games"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: international championship
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from different countries may participate. Called a "Championship"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: international cup
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from different countries may participate. Called a "Cup"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: international games
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from different countries may participate. Called "Games"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: national championship
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from one country may participate. Called a "Championship"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: national cup
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from one country may participate. Called a "Cup"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: national games
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from one country may participate. Called "Games"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: regional championship
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from a region of a country may participate. Called a "Championship"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: regional cup
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from a region of a country may participate. Called a "Cup"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: regional games
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from a region of a country may participate. Called "Games"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: world championship
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from the five continents may participate. Called "World Championship"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: world cup
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from the five continents may participate. Called "World Cup"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: world games
concept.definition: A competition where individuals or teams from the five continents may participate. Called "World Games"
concept.note: Retired December 2021.

concept.name: sport industry
concept.definition: Commercial issues related to sport

concept.name: sport organisation
concept.definition: Organisations or associations that govern sports

concept.name: sport venue
concept.definition: Gymnasiums, stadiums, arenas or facilities where sports events take place

concept.name: weather forecast
concept.definition: The long- or short-term meteorological prediction and reporting of the upcoming weather for a given region

concept.name: weather phenomena
concept.definition: Short- or long-term meteorological phenomena worthy of study, reporting or public advisory such as climate change, storms, heat waves or tornadoes

concept.name: weather statistic
concept.definition: Numerical facts about the weather such as temperature, barometric pressure, river levels, humidity or high and low tides

concept.name: weather warning
concept.definition: Alerts issued to the public about severe or notable weather in their area

concept.name: cultural policy
concept.definition: Government policies related to the promotion and support of the arts

concept.name: musical
concept.definition: A genre of music theatre in which singing and dancing play an essential part of the storytelling

concept.name: operetta
concept.definition: A genre of music theatre that usually has a light or humorous theme where some of the libretto is usually spoken rather than sung.

concept.name: art exhibition
concept.definition: Temporary presentation of art in museums, art halls or galleries

concept.name: trade show or expo
concept.definition: Market place to display and trade goods or services

concept.name: stock recommendation
concept.definition: An analyst's recommendation for particular stocks. May include the analyst’s price target for the stock.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000396 stocks and/or genre:Analysis instead.

concept.name: buy recommendation
concept.definition: An analyst's recommendation to buy particular stocks. May include the analyst’s price target for the stock.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000396 stocks and/or genre:Analysis instead.

concept.name: hold recommendation
concept.definition: An analyst's recommendation to hold particular stocks. May include the analyst’s price target for the stock.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000396 stocks and/or genre:Analysis instead.

concept.name: sell recommendation
concept.definition: An analyst's recommendation to sell particular stocks. May include the analyst’s price target for the stock.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000396 stocks and/or genre:Analysis instead.

concept.name: hot stock
concept.definition: Remarkable price movements of a particular stock.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000396 stocks and/or genre:Analysis instead.

concept.name: recreational hiking and climbing
concept.definition: Sportive activities in the mountains including climbing and hiking

concept.name: party
concept.definition: A social gathering of invited guests, often involving eating, drinking, dancing or the playing of games

concept.name: civilian service
concept.definition: Unpaid service for the community as civilian
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001277 volunteering instead.

concept.name: military service
concept.definition: Service by an individual in an army or militia. Service may be on a volunteer basis or a result of an involuntary draft or government requirement.

concept.name: integration policy
concept.definition: Improving the co-existence of groups of the society
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000634 immigration policy instead.

concept.name: sports policies
concept.definition: Government policies regulating sports, including sports betting, performance enhancing drug laws and the promition of sports to children for health purposes

concept.name: sports transaction
concept.definition: The transfer of an athlete from one team to another, the hiring or drafting of an athlete onto a team or the change in professional status of a player on a team

concept.name: cross-country skiing
concept.definition: Nordic ski race competed in relatively-flat, snowy countryside on narrow skis.
concept.note: Was retired but reinstated in August 2019

concept.name: Nordic combined
concept.definition: Competition combining cross-country skiing and ski jumping
concept.note: Was retired but reinstated in August 2019

concept.name: short track speed skating
concept.definition: Races competed by four or more skaters at a time on a small oval ice track

concept.name: artistic gymnastics
concept.definition: Gymnasts perform artistic and acrobatic moves on different apparatus. There are 6 events for men and 4 for women

concept.name: Telemark skiing
concept.definition: Races combining downhill and cross-country skiing events with unique Telemark techniques and equipment.

concept.name: chess
concept.definition: Competition between two players using a chequered board with 16 pieces to each side, each with specific ranges of movement depending on their identity

concept.name: roller sports
concept.definition: Various competitions using wheeled equipment such as roller skates, inline skates, and skateboards.

concept.name: skateboarding
concept.definition: Competitions involving stunts and acrobatic feats performed on a skateboard using various ramps and other obstacles

concept.name: martial arts
concept.definition: Martial arts are codified systems and traditions of combat practices, which are practiced for a variety of reasons: self-defence, competition, physical health and fitness, entertainment, as well as mental, physical, and spiritual development.

concept.name: start-up and entrepreneurial business
concept.definition: The process of designing, launching and running a new business

concept.name: public contract
concept.definition: Written contracts between companies and public authorities for the execution of works, the supply of products or the provision of services.

concept.name: handicrafts
concept.definition: The creators and sellers of crafts and the making and selling of the supplies used to make those crafts

concept.name: Internet of Things
concept.definition: A network of devices using microchips, electronic sensors and communication by the Internet
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000226 hardware, or generic medtop:20000225 computing and information technology as appropriate.

concept.name: asset management
concept.definition: Managing investments on behalf of others

concept.name: client relationship management
concept.definition: Creating and maintaining a relationship with the customers to increase sales.

concept.name: payment service
concept.definition: Services for executing payments including cash, cheque, credit card, debit card, direct debit, electronic funds transfer, internet banking, electronic and mobile payment.

concept.name: capital goods
concept.definition: Durable goods used in the production of other goods or services.

concept.name: railway manufacturing
concept.definition: Manufactures of rolling stock and suppliers for the maintenance and repair of railroads

concept.name: medical equipment
concept.definition: Manufacture of medical and surgical devices for diagnosis and treatment such as optical and imaging equipment

concept.name: pet product and service
concept.definition: Hygiene products, care and nutrition for pets

concept.name: e-cigarette
concept.definition: An electronic cigarette is a battery-operated device with an atomizer and a cartridge containing either a nicotine or a non-nicotine liquid solution.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000330 tobacco and nicotine instead.

concept.name: logistics
concept.definition: Supply chain, inventory management and transportation of products.

concept.name: sharing economy
concept.definition: Peer-to-peer-based sharing of access to goods and services

concept.name: small and medium enterprise
concept.definition: A small and medium enterprise (SME) is a company whose number of employees, balance and turnover cannot exceed certain limits set by the country where it is located

concept.name: regulatory authority
concept.definition: Authority responsible for the regulation or supervision of a business sector.
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000636 regulation of industry instead.

concept.name: regional development policy
concept.definition: Government or private policies, such as business incentives, aimed at the development of a region

concept.name: artistic swimming
concept.definition: A sport in which swimmers, in solo, duet or team configurations, perform coordinated or identical movements in time to music. They are judged and are awarded points for body position, control, and the degree of difficulty of the moves.

concept.name: rugby
concept.definition: A form of football developed in Rugby (England) played with an oval ball that may be kicked, carried and passed from hand to hand. Points are scored when one team carries the ball over the other team's goal line or kicks the ball between two goal posts.

concept.name: Olympic Games
concept.definition: Games organised by the International Olympic Committee and held each 4 years in summer or winter.

concept.name: Paralympic Games
concept.definition: Games for disabled athletes organised by the International Paralympic Committee and held each 4 years in winter or summer

concept.name: series
concept.definition: A connected set of fictional or non-fictional program episodes that run under the same title.

concept.name: drama (theatre)
concept.definition: A genre of theatre where dramatic or comedic plays are performed on the stage

concept.name: festival
concept.definition: Events showcasing artistic endeavours, often regularly occurring, such as dance, music, theatre, poetry or comedy festivals

concept.name: social media
concept.definition: Content created to be shared within online social networks

concept.name: eSports
concept.definition: Competitions involving video games played by professional gamers

concept.name: hip hop
concept.definition: A genre of music developed in African American communities in the United States in the 1970s which consists of rapping usually with an electronic backing

concept.name: r&b
concept.definition: A genre of music originated in African American communities in the 1940s and featuring soulful vocals

concept.name: heavy metal
concept.definition: A genre of loud, aggressive rock music that was developed in the late 1960s and early 1970s

concept.name: dance band music
concept.definition: A Swedish genre of music characterized by music inspired by swing, schlager, country, jazz and rock that is often danced to in pairs

concept.name: electronic dance music
concept.definition: A genre of music that employs electronic instruments and is primarily made for nightclubs, raves, and festivals

concept.name: children's music
concept.definition: A genre of music featuring songs composed and performed for children

concept.name: stand-up comedy
concept.definition: A comic style in which a comedian performs a series of jokes or stories in front of a live audience, usually speaking directly to them

concept.name: cabaret
concept.definition: A form of theatrical entertainment featuring music, song, dance, recitation, or drama that is performed in a nightclub or restaurant while the audience eats or drinks at tables

concept.name: influencers
concept.definition: Persons who distribute content on internet forums and social media and are followed by many people thereby influencing them to buy and use products

concept.name: sexual misconduct
concept.definition: Unwanted behaviour of a sexual nature that is of lesser offense than felony sexual assault, particularly when behaviour occurs in a normally non-sexual situation, or where there is some aspect of personal power or authority involved

concept.name: accounting crime
concept.definition: The intentional misrepresentation or alteration of accounting records regarding sales, revenues, expenses and other factors

concept.name: tax evasion
concept.definition: The illegal evasion of taxes by individuals, corporations, and trusts

concept.name: reckless driving
concept.definition: Serious traffic violations that are likely to, or have led to, the endangerment, injury or death of a person or persons, or the endangerment of or significant property damage

concept.name: administrative law
concept.definition: Body of law that governs the actions of governmental administrative agencies

concept.name: court-ordered psychiatric treatment
concept.definition: Psychiatric treatment imposed upon persons with mental health conditions in prisons and jails or as a condition of probation, supervision or parole

concept.name: suspended sentence
concept.definition: Legal arrangement where a judge delays the serving of a sentence by a person who has been found guilty of a crime in order to give them a probationary period

concept.name: dropped criminal investigation
concept.definition: When police and/or prosecutors end an investigation without a conclusion
concept.note: Moved from 20001200 (prosecution) to 20000131 (criminal investigation)

concept.name: wildlife traffic accident
concept.definition: Traffic accidents involving wild animals

concept.name: wildfire
concept.definition: An uncontrolled fire in an area of combustible vegetation

concept.name: textile arts
concept.definition: Arts and crafts that use plant, animal, or synthetic fibres to construct practical or decorative object

concept.name: woodworking
concept.definition: The activity or skill of making items, such as furniture or cabinets, from wood

concept.name: forging
concept.definition: The shaping of metal using localized compressive forces, such as heating and hammering

concept.name: self-employment
concept.definition: A situation in which an individual works for themselves as a freelancer or business owner instead of working for an employer that pays a salary or a wage

concept.name: biofuel
concept.definition: Fuel that is derived from biomass, such as ethanol

concept.name: geothermal power
concept.definition: Thermal energy that is generated and stored in the Earth

concept.name: solar power
concept.definition: Electricity generated and harvested from the sun's rays

concept.name: hydroelectric power
concept.definition: Electricity produced from hydropower when water flows through turbines

concept.name: wind power
concept.definition: Electricity that is generated by kinetic energy of wind, usually produced by windmills or turbines

concept.name: religious school
concept.definition: School that has a religious component in its operations or its curriculum

concept.name: state school
concept.definition: Schools maintained by public funds, usually government-imposed taxes

concept.name: private school
concept.definition: Schools maintained by private funds and organisations

concept.name: independent school
concept.definition: Schools funded by money from taxes via government, but run by companies, organisations or cooperatives

concept.name: vocational education
concept.definition: Education that provides students with practical experience and training in a particular occupational field. It is sometimes referred to as career education or technical education

concept.name: educational grading
concept.definition: The evaluation of a student's achievement on a test, assignment or course and the policies and methods around assigning those grades

concept.name: pandemic
concept.definition: An epidemic of infectious disease that has spread across a large region or even worldwide
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000449 epidemic and pandemic instead.

concept.name: drug rehabilitation
concept.definition: Medical or psychotherapeutic treatment for dependency on substances such as alcohol, prescription and controlled drugs

concept.name: general practice
concept.definition: The treatment of a wide range of acute and long-term medical conditions and symptoms
concept.note: Retired. Use general health terms instead.

concept.name: emergency care
concept.definition: Immediate care of patients with critical medical emergencies, either inside or outside of a hospital

concept.name: orthopaedics
concept.definition: A medical specialisation that focuses on conditions involving the musculoskeletal system
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000456 injury instead.

concept.name: oncology
concept.definition: A medical specialisation that focuses on the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of cancer
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000447 cancer instead.

concept.name: radiology
concept.definition: A medical specialisation that focuses on the use of medical imaging (such as x-rays) and radiation to diagnose and sometimes also treat diseases within the body
concept.note: Retired.

concept.name: eye care
concept.definition: The medical profession and field of study devoted to diagnosing, preventing and treating eye conditions

concept.name: surgical medicine
concept.definition: A medical specialisation that focuses on the treatment of injuries or disorders of the body by incision or manipulation, especially with instruments
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001228 surgery instead.

concept.name: psychiatry
concept.definition: A medical specialisation that focuses on the diagnosis, prevention and treatment of mental disorders
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000458 mental health instead.

concept.name: surgery
concept.definition: The use of incisions made by medical instruments to investigate or treat a medical condition or injury

concept.name: healthcare clinic
concept.definition: Local medical facility or doctor's office associated with a general medicine practice, staffed by one or many doctors, focusing on outpatient treatment or giving recommendations to visit a specialist or hospital

concept.name: environmental, social and governance policy (ESG)
concept.definition: A company's business practices reflecting responsible social, ethical and environmental policies, sometimes known as corporate social responsibility

concept.name: mixed martial arts
concept.definition: A full-contact combat sport using techniques from various combat sports and martial arts

concept.name: elderly care
concept.definition: The long-term care of the elderly provided by residential institutions or by paid daily help in the home

concept.name: name ceremony
concept.definition: Event at which an infant, youth, or adult is given a name

concept.name: graduation
concept.definition: A ceremony, also known as commencement, at which a person receives a diploma or academic degree

concept.name: funeral and memorial service
concept.definition: A ceremony to memorialise a deceased person

concept.name: wedding
concept.definition: Marriage ceremony and the events and parties surrounding it, such as wedding receptions, engagement parties, bridal showers and hen or stag parties

concept.name: anniversary
concept.definition: The celebration or commemoration of a significant amount of years since an event or a notable person's birth or death

concept.name: birthday
concept.definition: A celebration on the anniversary of a person's birth.

concept.name: exercise and fitness
concept.definition: Physical activity with the purpose to maintain and improve health and wellness, gain stamina or for enjoyment

concept.name: bodybuilding
concept.definition: Competitions where participants perform specific poses to display their muscles that have been strengthened and enlarged through strenuous exercise. Competitors are judged based on criteria such as symmetry, muscularity and conditioning.

concept.name: horseback riding
concept.definition: The riding of horses for leisure or light exercise.

concept.name: steeplechase (athletics)
concept.definition: Obstacle race in athletics including barriers and water jumps

concept.name: oil and gas
concept.definition: The exploration, extraction, refining, transporting and marketing of petroleum products and natural gas

concept.name: utilities
concept.definition: The providing of basic services to the public, such as supplying water, waste removal, heating and cooling

concept.name: infrastructure projects
concept.definition: The construction of the fundamental facilities and systems serving a country, city, or other area, including roads, bridges, dams, tunnels, telecommunications and electrical grids

concept.name: commercial building
concept.definition: The construction of buildings for commerce and business, including office buildings, retail shops, restaurants, hotels and stadiums
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20001367 commercial real estate instead.

concept.name: collecting
concept.definition: The hobby of seeking, locating, acquiring, organising, cataloguing, displaying, storing or maintaining items that are of interest to an individual collector

concept.name: cosmetics
concept.definition: Products applied to the body to enhance appearance, such as lipstick, eyeliner, foundation and nail polish, and the trends surrounding the application of these products

concept.name: hairstyles
concept.definition: Trends surrounding the cutting and styling of hair

concept.name: body modification
concept.definition: Actions taken to permanently or semi-permanently change body appearance, such as piercing and tattooing

concept.name: tattooing
concept.definition: A form of body modification where a design is made by inserting ink, dyes and pigments into the dermis layer of the skin to permanently change the pigment

concept.name: piercing
concept.definition: A form of body modification involving the puncturing or cutting of a part of the human body, creating an opening in which jewellery may be worn or where an implant could be inserted

concept.name: cooking and baking
concept.definition: Interest in preparing meals or baked goods

concept.name: beer, wine and spirits
concept.definition: Interest in the consumption, production and collection of alcoholic beverages

concept.name: home renovation
concept.definition: Interest in renovating one's property

concept.name: interior decoration
concept.definition: Interest in decorating one's home

concept.name: gardening
concept.definition: Interest in working in one's garden

concept.name: consumer electronics
concept.definition: Electronic devices, such as computers, smart phones or televisions, designed and sold for personal use

concept.name: political party convention
concept.definition: Meeting of political party to nominate a candidate, choose a party leader or set policy

concept.name: political committee
concept.definition: A legislative sub-group that focuses on a specific area or action

concept.name: head of government
concept.definition: The highest official in the executive branch of a political entity

concept.name: public inquiry
concept.definition: An official review of events or actions ordered by a government body, including royal commissions

concept.name: church elections
concept.definition: Elections to choose leaders to run church organisations at various levels
concept.note: Retired. Use medtop:20000703 religious leader instead.

concept.name: infrastructure policy
concept.definition: Policies regarding the development and maintenance of the fundamental facilities of a country, city or other area, such as communication, transportation and distribution networks

concept.name: local government policy
concept.definition: Policies for the development and maintenance of a local government area

concept.name: political debates
concept.definition: Organised debates between political candidates during their election campaign

concept.name: Midsummer
concept.definition: Northern European celebrations that accompany the Summer solstice

concept.name: National day
concept.definition: A designated day to celebrate a nation

concept.name: New year
concept.definition: The celebration of the end of one calendar year and the start of next

concept.name: Halloween
concept.definition: A celebration observed in a number of countries on 31 October with activities including trick-or-treating, wearing costumes, carving pumpkins, visiting haunted attractions, telling scary stories and watching horror films

concept.name: All Saints Day
concept.definition: Christian festival celebrated in honour of all the saints, known and unknown. Holiday is also known as All Hallows' Day, Hallowmas, Feast of All Saints or Solemnity of All Saints.

concept.name: Walpurgis night
concept.definition: The eve of the Christian feast day of Saint Walpurga, an 8th-century abbess in Francia, celebrated on the night of 30 April and the day of 1 May

concept.name: baptism
concept.definition: A Christian religious rite, sometimes also called a Christening, involving the sprinkling of holy water onto a person's forehead or immersing them in water, symbolising purification and admission to the Church

concept.name: contraception
concept.definition: A method or device used to prevent pregnancy

concept.name: gig economy
concept.definition: a labour market consisting of individual suppliers on short-term contracts or freelance work as opposed to permanent jobs, such as drivers or delivery workers

concept.name: parental leave
concept.definition: Time off work to give birth or care for a child

concept.name: volunteering
concept.definition: Unpaid work undertaken freely by individuals as a service to others

concept.name: cooperative
concept.definition: An organisation which is owned and run jointly by its members, who share the profits or benefits of their work

concept.name: cryptocurrency
concept.definition: An internet-based medium of exchange using cryptography to conduct financial transactions

concept.name: hurricane
concept.definition: A type of storm also called a tropical cyclone, which forms over tropical or subtropical waters

concept.name: racquetball
concept.definition: A racquet sport similar to squash in which the ball must bounce once before being struck and with a greater area of in-bound play

concept.name: ringette
concept.definition: A team sport usually played on ice by skaters using a straight stick. Players pass and shoot a rubber ring in order to place it in the opponent's goal

concept.name: human smuggling and trafficking
concept.definition: The illegal transporting of people from one country or area to another. When the result is forced labour or sexual exploitation, the crime is considered trafficking.

concept.name: bullying
concept.definition: Taking actions meant to harm, coerce and intimidate another person perceived as vulnerable. These actions can be taken in person or online.

concept.name: vandalism
concept.definition: Deliberately damaging or destroying public or private property

concept.name: arm wrestling
concept.definition: A form of wrestling in which two participants grapple arm-to-arm

concept.name: supreme and high court
concept.definition: matters related to supreme or high courts, including nominations and rulings

concept.name: indigenous people
concept.definition: People who are the original owners and caretakers of a given region, also known as native peoples, first peoples or aboriginal peoples, in contrast to groups that have settled, occupied or colonised the area more recently

concept.name: sales channel
concept.definition: Indirect or direct ways of bringing products or services to market so that they can be purchased by consumers

concept.name: public housing
concept.definition: Housing that is owned or managed by a government or nonprofit organisation and rented to tenants with the aim of making housing affordable. Eligibility for such housing arrangements varies country to country.

concept.name: heating and cooling
concept.definition: The business of supplying heat or cooling to private residences and commercial buildings

concept.name: economic development incentive
concept.definition: The offer of funds, financial incentive programmes or subsidies to stimulate businesses and the economy

concept.name: streaming service
concept.definition: Providers of entertainment (music, movies, series, etc.) that deliver the content via an internet connection to the subscriber's computer, TV or mobile device

concept.name: crowdfunding
concept.definition: The funding of a project or venture by raising small amounts of money from a large number of people, typically via the internet

concept.name: outsourcing
concept.definition: Business practice in which services or job functions are farmed out to a third party

concept.name: shopping mall
concept.definition: A large building or series of connected buildings that contain a variety of retail stores and food vendors

concept.name: shopping district and high street
concept.definition: Primary retail streets of towns or cities containing individual stores and restaurants

concept.name: artificial intelligence
concept.definition: An attempt to let computers simulate how the human brain works by applying statistical, heuristic or machine learning methods and algorithms to large datasets. Enables a long range of automatic functions like image recognition and categorisation.

concept.name: surveillance
concept.definition: Monitoring the activities of the individual through the use of cameras and data in order to prevent or uncover criminal activities

concept.name: privacy
concept.definition: The right of an individual or group to keep information about themselves private

concept.name: sport achievement
concept.definition: Records or honours earned by athletes for their performance

concept.name: sports record
concept.definition: The best performance to date by an athlete or team in a sport discipline

concept.name: sports medal and trophy
concept.definition: Award given to an athlete or team for placing in a sports competition, such as a gold medal at the Olympics or the Stanley Cup in ice hockey

concept.name: sports honour
concept.definition: Honour given to a player for achievements over a career, season or single event, unrelated to event outcome, usually awarded by a panel of judges or a popular vote. Examples include player of the game, most valuable player award or hall of fame induction.

concept.name: padel
concept.definition: Racquet sport typically played in doubles on an enclosed court

concept.name: kiting
concept.definition: Action sport where a person harnesses the power of the wind to propel themselves across water, land or snow on a board, snowboard or skis. Variations of the sport are called kiteboarding/kitesurfing, snowkiting or landkiting.

concept.name: stand up paddleboarding (SUP)
concept.definition: Water sport using a board and paddle to propel through the water while standing

concept.name: krav maga
concept.definition: Fighting and self-defence sport that combines elements from aikido, boxing, judo, karate and wrestling

concept.name: cheerleading
concept.definition: Cheer squads competing against each other to perform a routine that includes stunts, jumps and tumbling set to music. Squads are judged on difficulty and execution.

concept.name: kickboxing
concept.definition: Stand-up combat sport based on kicking and punching an opponent

concept.name: sambo (martial art)
concept.definition: Style of martial art originating in the Soviet Union that is recognised as a form of amateur wrestling

concept.name: political leadership
concept.definition: Positions of leadership within political parties and discussion around whether leadership changes need to be made

concept.name: shootings
concept.definition: An act of violence committed by individuals with the use of armed weapons

concept.name: animal abuse
concept.definition: The infliction of cruelty or neglect onto animals by humans

concept.name: zoo
concept.definition: A park where visitors can see both wild and tame animals that are kept in cages or enclosed areas

concept.name: border dispute
concept.definition: Diplomatic issues between two countries regarding territorial borders

concept.name: child care
concept.definition: Services and facilities that look after children while their parents are working or away, including daycare centres and programmes

concept.name: pest and pest control
concept.definition: Animals or insects that invade a human space causing destruction or unsanitary conditions and the methods for controlling or eradicating them
concept.note: For pesticides, see medtop:20001395 under chemicals

concept.name: disinformation and misinformation
concept.definition: The spread of false information regardless of intent

concept.name: torture
concept.definition: Intentionally inflicting severe physical or psychological pain in order to punish the victim or get the victim to take an action or give information

concept.name: drowning
concept.definition: When a person's airpipe and lungs become filled with water causing death or injury, most often caused by falling into or bathing in rivers, lakes or the ocean

concept.name: poisoning
concept.definition: When a substance in sufficient quantity adversely affects the body and its functions

concept.name: sports coaching
concept.definition: The staff responsible for the training and on-field management of a sports team

concept.name: sports management and ownership
concept.definition: The executive leadership and owners of a sports team

concept.name: sports officiating
concept.definition: Referees, umpires and other staff who enforce the rules of a sport from on the field of play

concept.name: women's rights
concept.definition: The rights of women to be treated equally as men, such as pay parity, feminism and bodily autonomy around abortion
concept.note: See also medtop:20000779 sexism

concept.name: women
concept.definition: People who identify as female, focusing on the group as a demographic, and accomplishments, such as the first woman to achieve a milestone or serve in a role

concept.name: men
concept.definition: People who identify as male, focusing on the group as a demographic, and masculinity

concept.name: 3x3 basketball
concept.definition: A variation of basketball played three a side on one basketball hoop

concept.name: canoe slalom
concept.definition: Competitors navigate a canoe or a kayak on a white-water course, passing through a combination of upstream and downstream gates on river rapids in the fastest time possible

concept.name: canoe sprint
concept.definition: Taking place on a flatwater course, multiple canoes take off at once and race each other to the finish

concept.name: bmx freestyle
concept.definition: Riders are given a fixed time to perform acrobatics tricks and skills, with tricks scored on multiple aspects including difficulty, originality, execution, height and creativity

concept.name: road cycling
concept.definition: Cycling events held on roads, including road races and time trials

concept.name: track cycling
concept.definition: Cycling competitions held on tracks, including sprint, team sprint, keirin, team pursuit, omnium and madison events

concept.name: football 5-a-side
concept.definition: A variation of association football in which each team fields five players and play is on a smaller pitch. An official Summer Paralympics sport.

concept.name: goalball
concept.definition: A team sport for athletes with a vision impairment, using a ball containing bells

concept.name: online and remote learning
concept.definition: Learning where a student and teacher are not physically present in the same location. Instruction can occur synchronously or asynchronously and utilizes technology such as video conferencing, online assessments and video conferencing.

concept.name: education policy
concept.definition: Government programmes and policies related to education, such as the funding of schools and standardised testing
concept.note: Moved from medtop:05000000 to medtop:20000621

concept.name: wellness
concept.definition: The active pursuit of good mental and physical health

concept.name: mental wellbeing
concept.definition: The personal pursuit of mental health through activities such as meditation, stress reduction, technology detoxes and journalling

concept.name: regular competition
concept.definition: A competitive sporting season where teams or individuals contend for a place in the standings or rankings

concept.name: playoff championship
concept.definition: A competitive tournament, often played after the regular season of a sports league by the top competitors to determine the league champion or a similar accolade. Can also apply to an all-in elimination tournament meant to produce a final champion.

concept.name: final game
concept.definition: Single game that determines the winner of a championship, such as the Super Bowl or Champions League Final

concept.name: Catholicism
concept.definition: Largest Christian religion in the world encompassing the Roman Catholic Church which is led by the pope, and Old Catholicism, a breakaway movement founded in 1870 that denies the dogma of infallibility of the pope

concept.name: bar and bat mitzvah
concept.definition: Young person becoming an adult in the Jewish faith

concept.name: canonisation
concept.definition: The declaration of a deceased person as an officially recognised saint

concept.name: Shia Islam
concept.definition: The second largest branch of Islam believing that the Prophet Muhammad designated Ali ibn Abi Talib as his successor

concept.name: Sunni Islam
concept.definition: The largest branch of Islam believing that the Prophet Muhammad did not designate a successor

concept.name: atheism and agnosticism
concept.definition: The belief that god does not exist or that it is impossible to know whether a god exists

concept.name: Eid al-Adha
concept.definition: Muslim festival marking the culmination of the annual hajj pilgrimage and commemorating the willingness of the Prophet Ibrahim to sacrifice his son

concept.name: Hasidism
concept.definition: Jewish religious group that believes that the Torah is the literal word of God and that carrying out this word is what gives meaning and purpose to life

concept.name: Hanukkah
concept.definition: Jewish festival, sometimes spelled Chanukah, observed by lighting the candles of a menorah

concept.name: tsunami
concept.definition: High and powerful ocean waves caused by an underwater land disturbance, such as an earthquake or volcanic eruption, known to cause significant damage and loss when they hit land

concept.name: healthcare industry
concept.definition: Goods and services to treat patients through curative, preventive, rehabilitative and palliative care

concept.name: developmental disorder
concept.definition: Conditions due to an impairment in learning, language or behaviour areas, such as autism, Aspergers and dyslexia

concept.name: depression
concept.definition: A mental health disorder characterised by persistently depressed mood and loss of interest in activities, causing significant impairment in daily life

concept.name: anxiety and stress
concept.definition: A mental health disorder characterised by feelings of worry, anxiousness or fear that are strong enough to interfere with one's daily activities

concept.name: public health
concept.definition: Organised public or private measures designed to prevent disease, promote health and prolong life among the population as a whole

concept.name: pregnancy and childbirth
concept.definition: Social and medical issues around pregnancy and giving birth including, doulas and midwives, obstetrics and medical concerns

concept.name: fraternal and community group
concept.definition: Social organisations whose members freely associate for a mutually beneficial purpose such as for social, professional or honorary principles. Groups include Rotary clubs, Lions clubs or Freemasons.

concept.name: cyber warfare
concept.definition: Cyber attacks, or suspected attacks, involving a government entity as either the sponsor or the target, or preventive measures against these types of actions

concept.name: public transport
concept.definition: Forms of transportation, such as buses and trains, that charge set fares, run on fixed routes and are available to the general public

concept.name: taxi and ride-hailing
concept.definition: On-demand ride service, provided by taxi companies or tech companies such as Uber and Lyft, the drivers who transport passengers and the rules and regulations imposed upon these companies within a region

concept.name: shared transport
concept.definition: A network of vehicles, such as cars, bikes and scooters, that can be rented by travellers on an as-needed basis for short-term use

concept.name: business reporting and performance
concept.definition: Measuring and reporting on the performance of a business

concept.name: business restructuring
concept.definition: Changes to the structure of a business or organisation

concept.name: commercial real estate
concept.definition: The building and sale of property used for business purposes rather than as a living space

concept.name: residential real estate
concept.definition: The building and sale of property to be used as living space

concept.name: podcast
concept.definition: Podcast networks and the people who create and distribute audio content, typically in a series format, to audiences

concept.name: financial service
concept.definition: Professional services involving the investment, lending or management of money and other assets

concept.name: business service
concept.definition: Services provided to business operations and for individuals, such as consulting, legal representation and job placement

concept.name: news industry
concept.definition: The production and distribution of content about newsworthy events through print, broadcast, video or electronic media

concept.name: diversity, equity and inclusion
concept.definition: Efforts that seeks to promote the fair treatment and full participation of groups of people who have historically been underrepresented or subject to discrimination on the basis of identity or disability

concept.name: sustainability
concept.definition: Actions by organizations, governments, and individuals in response to environmental problems

concept.name: profit sharing
concept.definition: A system by which employees are paid a share of the net profits of their employer. These earnings are distinct from and additional to regular wages.

concept.name: corporate bond
concept.definition: Corporate paper representing the loan of money at a fixed rate and for a fixed time to that company or government

concept.name: war victims
concept.definition: Individuals who are injured or killed in a war or armed conflict, including civilians

concept.name: missing in action
concept.definition: A member of the armed forces who cannot be located and has not been confirmed as either alive or dead
concept.note: Duplicate of medtop:20000061, added in error. Please do not use.

concept.name: breaking (breakdance)
concept.definition: Dancing competition featuring an urban dance style that originated in the United States in the 1970s with roots in hip-hop culture and characterised by acrobatic movements, stylised footwork, and the key role played by the DJ and the MC during battles

concept.name: by-election
concept.definition: An election used to fill an office that has become vacant between general elections because of the death of a politician, a resignation or other reason

concept.name: recall election
concept.definition: A special election to vote to remove an elected official from office before that official's term has ended

concept.name: coalition building
concept.definition: Political parties entering a power-sharing arrangement usually occurring when no single party has achieved an absolute majority after an election

concept.name: zoning policy
concept.definition: Polices around the division of land into zones, each of which has a set of regulations for new development

concept.name: political party
concept.definition: Organisation that coordinates candidates to run to represent that party in an election

concept.name: political movement and association
concept.definition: A collective of people with the aim to change government policy or social values in opposition to the status quo

concept.name: communism
concept.definition: Form of government that aims to replace private property and a profit-based economy with public ownership and communal control of the means of production and the natural resources within in society

concept.name: democratic socialism
concept.definition: Form of government that consists of a socialist economy in which the means of production are socially and collectively owned or controlled alongside a democratic political system of government with officials elected to office by citizens

concept.name: theocracy
concept.definition: Form of government in which authority comes from a religion and the leaders are thought to be divinely guided

concept.name: absolute monarchy
concept.definition: Form of government in which a person, who is part of a hereditary line, reigns as head of state for life or until abdication

concept.name: ski mountaineering
concept.definition: A winter sport that combines skiing and mountaineering that involves climbing mountains on skis or by foot and then skiing down

concept.name: vegetarianism and veganism
concept.definition: The avoidance of meat and other animal products for moral, dietary or religious reasons and the plant-based products that adhere to this principle

concept.name: park and playground
concept.definition: A public green area in a town or city used for recreation

concept.name: futsal
concept.definition: A football-based game played indoors on a hard court with five players per team

concept.name: beach soccer
concept.definition: A football-based game played indoors on a hard court with five players per team

concept.name: pesticide and herbicide
concept.definition: Chemicals used to eliminate weeds and pests

schemeMeta.name: Media Topic
schemeMeta.definition: Indicates a subject of an item.
schemeMeta.note: The Media Topic NewsCodes has been IPTC's primary subject taxonomy since 2010, with a focus on classification of text. The development started with our previous Subject Codes taxonomy and extended the tree to 5 levels and reused the same 17 top level terms. The terms below the top level have been revised and rearranged. Most Media Topic concepts provide a mapping back to one of the Subject Codes, and many provide a mapping to Wikidata.
